ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Bunso zaa piiligu, Naawunni n daa n maalini saazu ni tiŋŋa.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Tiŋŋa maa daa n niŋini binyoo, ka ligu ni koom paali u, Naawunni sii poli n jinna koom maa.

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawun yeli, “Cheli paaligu ta.” Lala mi niŋi.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: O nye paaligu maa, ka o veela. O barigi paaligu maa yi ligu la niŋi.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: O booli paaligu maa bee, ka booli ligu la mi yiiŋu. Zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi o dapiiligu.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli bobiri barigi koom maa buyi.

ref: And that's what happened. God made the dome
text: Bobiri maa ti seesi du saa, ka seesi ba tiŋŋa.”

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Ka Naawunni booli bobiri maa sagbaŋŋu. O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba ayi daari.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli koseesi n ba tiŋŋa laŋsi ziluŋko, ka tiŋgbaŋŋu kuuŋu o ta.” Lala mi niŋi.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: O booli tiŋgbaŋŋu kuuŋu la tiŋŋa, ka kolaŋsira la mini teeku. O nyaasi a, ka a veela.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli tiŋgbaŋŋu buli bumbula, seesi a niŋi chi baligu, ka seesi a niŋi tiisi baligu, ka a wanna.” A mi niŋi.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Lala n na tiŋgbaŋŋu maa buli bumbula balisi. Naawunni nyaasi a, ka a veela.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba ata daari.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli paaligusi ta sagbana niŋi, a barigi bee ni yiiŋu, jemani, daasi, a ni yuma.

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: A zaa ni ba sagbana niŋi, a tira tiŋgbaŋŋu maa paaligu.” A mi niŋi.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Lala n na, Naawunni maali paaligu karigu ni paaligu bila, ŋa la o tira bee paaligu, ŋa la o tira yiiŋu paaligu. Ka o maali ŋmeribiisi gba.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: A zaa ba sagbana niŋi tira tiŋgbaŋŋu maa paaligu,

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: a nyaasira bee ni yiiŋu, ka barigi paaligu yi ligu niŋi. Naawunni nyaasi a, ka a veela.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba anaasi daari.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli koom puu bunvuusira baligu zaa, a zoo n ba niŋi chaŋina. Ka cheli binyigira zaa, a ba niŋi yigira sagbana zu.”

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Naawunni daa n maali binnyaara kara niŋi koom puwa, a ni koom puu binnyaara zaa, a zoo n chaŋina, a ni niinsi balisi zaa. Naawunni nyaasi a, ka a veela.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Naawunni nafa a, ka yeli, “Ya wasi n paali koom maa niŋi. Ka niinsi gba, a wasi.”

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba anu daari.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli tiŋgbaŋŋu binnyaara ni a balisi zaa ba niŋi, yiri buŋkobiri, moo puwa buŋkobiri, a ni binnyaara zaa.” A mi niŋi.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Naawunni maali a zaa, ka nyaasi a, ka a veela.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli ti maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani ti, ka mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, niinsi, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu buŋkobiri zaa niŋi.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawunni maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani u. O maali ba daba ni poga.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Naawunni nafa ba, ka yeli, “Ya dogira biisi, a paali tiŋgbaŋŋu maa, ka nyaasira u, a mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, saazu niinsi, a ni bini zaa n chaŋina tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Ya nyaasi, m ti ya ni chi baligu, a ni tiisi wala, a niŋi ya bindira.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: M naani moori ni vaari ti buŋkobiri, niinsi a ni tiŋvuurigusi, a niŋi a bindira.” Lala mi niŋi.

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Ka Naawunni nyaasi bunso zaa o n maali maa, ka a veela ga. O zaanoori ni boguuniŋi n daa n niŋi daba ayoobu daari.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: N ŋa n na saazu ni tiŋŋa ni bunso zaa n ba a niŋi daa n maali.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Daba ayapoin daari Naawunni tuma maa baasi, ka o naa dindaari maa vuusi.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Naawunni nafa daba ayapoin daari maa, ka loobi u vuusim daa, a kpali bo, o tuma maa zaa baasiya.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Seema Naawunni n daa n maali saazu ni tiŋŋa n na la. Seema Naawunni n maali nisaala n ŋa Duuma Naawunni n daa maali saazu ni tiŋŋa zaa la,

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: vatuya ni bumbula daa na ka niŋi, a kpali Duuma Naawunni na bi ti saa ta, ka nira zaa daa n na ka, o ko tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: Ama koom daa n bulisini yi tiŋŋa, a du n maagira tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: A jemani maa Duuma Naawunni naa tanni me daba, ka vuusi nyevuri vuusim niŋi o ŋoya niŋi, o loobi nyevuri daana.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Ka ti Duuma Naawunni digili pu, a yi Idin paaligu n ba wuluŋu pusigu, ka naa daba maa niŋi nisaaniŋi.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni ti tisuma zaa baligu buli, seesi wala n veela buli. Tiisi ayi za pu maa sunsuuniŋi, ŋala tira nyevuri, ka ŋala mi tira yasuma ni yabeeri baŋinisim.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Mogiri mi daa n bani Idin paaligu, a zo n ti maagira pu maa, ka barigi bunaasi.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: Piiligu mogiri maa ba boona Pison, o zo n wara Havila paaligu zaa.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: Sugasuma mi ba niŋi, a ni tulalesuma, a ni kuguso n mari tono.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Moga ayi daana maa n niŋi Gihon, o mi zo n wara Kusi paaligu zaa.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Moga atadaana maa n niŋi Tigirisi, a zo n yiira Asiriya paaligu n ba wuluŋu pusigu. Moga anaasidaana maa n niŋi Yufireeti.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Duuma Naawunni n naa daba maa niŋi pu la n ba Idin paaligu la o doosira, ka nyaasira u.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Ka ti u zaaligu yeli, “E ni tee n di pu maa tiisi zaa wala,

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: ama, da pa ti di tiso n tira yasuma ni yabeeri baŋinisim la wala. A kpali bo, dindaso zaa e n di u, e ni kpi.”

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni yeli, “O bi daga daba ko n ba niŋi, m ni maali suŋina, o niŋi o todaana.”

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni ti daba maa gbisi ga. O n gbisira maa, Duuma Naawunni nyisi o nyekobiri, ka ti nisaaniŋi ti yo.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: O naa nyekobiri maa maali poga, a ti daba maa.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: A nyaaŋa daba maa yeli, “Pimparaŋa wune m pa nye m gbagba ŋmaani, m koba niŋi kobiri, m valiga niŋi valiga. O n yi daba niŋi zu, o yuuri n niŋi poga.”

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Lala zu, daba yi cheli o ba ni o ma, ka naa poga, ba loobi luŋko.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Daba maa ni poga maa daa n marini alibana, ka vi bi mari ba.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: A jemani maa, waafu n daa n baŋŋi yomsigu gaari buŋkobiri zaa Naawunni n daa n maali la. Dindaso o daa n ta poga maa saani, a ti boosi u, “Yamonsi n na Naawunni yeli ya da dira tii zaa wala pu ŋa niŋi?”

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Poga maa yeli u, “Ti n tee n di tii zaa wala n ba pu ŋa niŋi.

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: Ama tiso n za sunsuuniŋi la, Naawunni yeli ti da pa ti di o wala, koo a siisi u gba, ka pa lala, ti ni kpi.”

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: Waafu maa yeli, “Akai, ya ki kpi.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: Naawunni baŋiya maa, ka ya di u, ya nufu ni yoo, ya ba maa Naawunni, a baŋŋi yasuma ni yabeeri.”

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Niŋi n na, poga maa nyaasi, ka tii maa wala veela diibu, a niŋi u naŋgbaŋkuubu maa o n di n di u, ka mari yam. A zu, o po u di, ka naa n ti o sira gba o di.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Noba maa niŋi, ba nufu yoo, ba nye maa ba bi so sen, a zu ba naa tii vayelima se n tomi taaba, a so.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Dindaari maa zaanomaasiri, ba wumi Duuma Naawunni n chaŋina pu maa niŋi, ba zo n sogira tiisi maa puwa.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni booli daba maa, “Ya n na e be?”

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Daba maa yeli, “M wumini e n chaŋina pu la niŋi, dabeem gbaa ma, a kpali ni mari alibana zu, ma sogi.”

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Naawunni boosi u, “Ŋani n yeli i ni e marini alibana? E dini tiso ni yeli e da di la wala la?”

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Daba maa yeli u, “Poga la e n naa ti ma la n ti ma tii maa walli ma di.”

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Duuma Naawunni boosi poga maa, “Bo n na e niŋi lala?” Poga maa yeli u, “Waafu la n yomsi ma, ma di u.”

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni yeli waafu maa “A kpali bunso e n niŋi a zu, a yi buŋkobiri zaa puwa, e ko ni nye n ŋa naŋgbansiibu, za zinaa wara e ni naa e puu vuura tiŋŋa, ka e dira tanni, a yi e nyevuri niŋi.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: M ni ti dintiri o ba e ni poga maa sunsuuniŋi, ka e biisi ni o biisi gba ni niŋi dindima. O bii ni zaasi e zu, ka e mi dumi o nosiŋkpaari.”

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli poga maa, “M ni ti e mari sufusaaŋu, ka nye beerim, dindaso e n ya n dogi. Ŋama nyaaŋa, e sira yela ni ba e sufu niŋi ga, ka o mini n ni niŋi e zudaana.”

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Niŋi n na, o yeli daba maa gba, “A kpali e ni wumi n ti e poga, ka di tiwaliso ni yeli ya da di la zu, m si tiŋgbaŋaa noori, ka pa e tumi n beligi ga, ka nye bindirigu, a yi e beebu puwa.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni buli matimari ni goosi, ka ya dira moo niŋi tiisi wala.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: E ni tumi ga, a nyee zibiiŋu, e bindirigu zu, a yi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ka e lee n pori n kpe tanni maa niŋi. Tanni ba naa n me i, e ni lee n pori n loobi tanni.”

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Adam digili o poga maa yuuri Hawa, o gbinni n niŋi nisaaladima zaa ma.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Ka Duuma Naawunni naa buŋkobiri gbana maali bimpinna pili Adam ni o poga.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma Naawunni yeli, “Pimparaŋa nisaala pa loobini maa tirima, a baŋŋi yasuma ni yabeeri. A bi daga ti n cheli o ba kpe, ka tee n nye soori n lee n di tiso n tira nyevuri la walli, ka ki lee n kpi, ka ba niŋi n wara.”

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: A zu, Duuma Naawunni nyisi u yi pu maa n ba Idin paaligu la, o wa ti koora tiŋgbanso o n ŋma n me u la niŋi.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: O n nyisi u yi pu maa niŋi la, o ti malaakasi ni tokobiso n leegira bugum poli n jinna, pu maa wuluŋpusigu, a kogira nisaaniŋi, nira zaa o da nye soori ta nyevuri tii maa ziya.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Adam ni o poga lagimi, o mari puwa, a dogi daba. Niŋi n na Hawa yeli, “Naawunni n suŋŋi ma, ma dogi daba, ka digili o yuuri Keen.”

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: A nyaaŋa, Hawa lee n dogi daba, Ebel. Ebel daa niŋini buŋkobguuna, ka Keen mi niŋi kokpaara.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: A n daa n ti paa jemani yinni, Keen mari o pu bindira, a ti puusi Naawunni.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: Ka Ebel mi ku o pee bipiiligu, ka naa o zimaŋa ziya puusi Naawunni, a peri Naawunni ga, o deeg o puusigu.

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: Ama, Keen ni o puusigu daa n bi peri Naawunni, suuri gbaa Keen.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Duuma boosi Keen, “Bo zu n na e je suuri, a dugu nufu?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Ka e di niŋi bunso n daga, m di ki deeg e puusigu? Ama, e n bi niŋi nene la zu, yabeeri kpabirini o nyaŋŋi i, ama, a daga e nyaŋŋi u.”

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
text: Dindaso, Keen yeli o sunzo Ebel, “Cheli ti gaa laŋgaraa.” Ba n we nisaaniŋi, Keen ŋme u ku.

ref: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma boosi Keen, “E sunzo ba ya?” Keen yeli, “M bi baŋŋi. Mani n nyaasira u?”

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Duuma boosi u, “Bo tumtumbee n na e tumi a? E sunzo zeem do tiŋŋa chaari n tira ma, a maa, m darigi e tubiri.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: A zu, naŋgbansiibu niŋini e toori, e ki lee n tee n ko o niŋi, e sunzo zeem la zu.

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Keen yeli Duuma, “M tubidarigirisimaa ba toom ga ti ma.

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: Zinni, e kari ma ni yi e saani, a ni tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi. M ki mari yiri, ka gaa n chaŋina. Ka nivu zaa paa ma, o n ku ma.”

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Duuma yeli u, “Akai, ka nira zaa ku i, ni ku o niriba bayapoin yo samini.” Lala zu, Naawunni duligi Keen, a kpaasi niriba tuba, a maa ba da ku u.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: A zu, Keen yi Naawunni saani, a we paaliguso ba n boona Nodi, o gbinni n niŋi, gaa n chaŋina, a yi Idin wuluŋpusigu.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Niŋi n na, Keen naa poga, a dogi biya, ka digili o yuuri Inoki. Ka me tiŋtintaa, ka naa bii maa yuuri digili tiŋŋa maa.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: A jemani maa, Adam ni o poga lee n dogi daba. Poga maa yeli, “Naawunni lee n ti ma ni biduwa, o za Ebel noba niŋi, so Keen n daa n ku la.” O digili o yuuri Sefi.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Niŋi n na, Sefi gba naa poga, a dogi daba, ka digili o yuuri Inosi. A jemani maa n na niriba piili n naa Duuma yuuri kpeesira u.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Adam yuma kobsiwai ni pisita nyaaŋa n na o kpi.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Sefi yuma kobsiwai ni pii ni ayi, ka o kpi.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Inosi dini yuma kobsiwai ni anu ka kpi.

ref: When Kenan was
text: Keenan yuma pisiyapoin ka dogi Mahalali.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Keenan daa n dini yuma kobsiwai ni piiya, ka kpi.

text: Duuma yeli, “M sisuŋŋu ki ba nisaala niŋi, o ba niŋi wara. O n niŋi valiga la zu, o yuugirisim zaa o niŋi yuma koowa ni pisiyi ko.”

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.

ref: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Niŋi n na, Nuhu niŋi bunso zaa Naawunni n yeli u la.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: A kpali bo, m ya n ti ni koom paali durinye zaa, a muri bunvuusira zaa nyem.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

text: Ka e laŋsi bindira baligu zaa niŋi ŋariŋu maa puwa, a niŋi ya ni buŋkobiri maa bindira.”

ref: Seven days from now I will send rain that will last for
text: Daba ayapoin nyaaŋa, m ni ti saa ta dapisinaasi, wuntoona ni yiina, a muri bunvuusigu zaa ni maali durinye ŋa puwa”.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Daba ayapoin maa nyaaŋa, komutugu maa piili.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.

ref: They took along every kind of animal, tame and wild, including the birds.
text: Ka ba mari buŋkobiri balisi zaa gba paasi n kpe ŋariŋu maa.

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Binnyaara zaa baligu ayaayi gba paasi Nuhu niŋi kpe ŋariŋu maa.

ref: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
text: Bunvuusira maa daa niŋini daba ni poga, a maa seema Duuma Naawunni n daa yeli la. Nuhu ni bini maa zaa n kpe n baasi, Duuma gbagba yo ŋarintintaa maa noori.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Koom maa n daa n paali n za, daasi koowa ni pisinu.

ref: God did not forget about Noah and the animals with him in the boat. So God made a wind blow, and the water started going down.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni daa n bi tami Nuhu ni buŋkobiri la zaa n daa n ba ŋariŋu maa puu la yela, o ti sauwa vaara, koom maa piili n sigira.

ref: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
text: O ti konimbiisi zaa n daa n ba tiŋŋa bulisira la zaa nyeri, ka yo saa dinaŋgbana, saa maa kasigi.

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Dapii ni ayapoin daari, ŋmerisi ayapoin daana niŋi, ŋariŋu maa sigi n ti za zoso ba n boona Ararat la zukpaa niŋi.

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: Koom maa sigira, a ti paa ŋmerisi pii daana dayinni, zoori yi n turi.

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: Ka tumi nyiŋgbuŋ, o bi lee n pori n ta, ka ba niŋi n yigi n poli n jinna, a ti paa koom maa n ŋu.

ref: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.
text: Niŋi n na, Nuhu lee n tumi ŋmenni ni o yi n nyaasi n nyee koom maa sigiya a.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: Ŋmenni maa daa n bi nye ziya o dobi, a kpali koom maa daa n na n paali maa zu, o pori n ta ŋariŋu maa ziya, Nuhu teeg o nuu gbaag u niŋi.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: O guri daba ayapoin nyaaŋa, o lee n tumi ŋmenni.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Dindaari maa zaanoori, ŋmenni maa pori n ta ka tiso ba n boona olivi la vapaali kagini o noori. Niŋi n na, Nuhu baŋŋi maa koom maa sigira.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: O lee n guri daba ayapoin, ka lee tumi ŋmenni maa gbagba, ŋala wune ŋmenni maa bi lee n pori n ta.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: O ŋmerisi ayi daana dapisiyi ni ayapoin n na tiŋŋa daa kuu nyem.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni yeli Nuhu,

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: “Pimparaŋa, e ni e poga ni ya biduusi ni ba pogiba ya yi ŋariŋu maa puwa.

ref: Let out the birds, animals, and reptiles, so they can mate and live all over the earth.”
text: Nyisi niinsi ni bunvuusira zaa n ba ŋariŋu la puu la paasi n yi, a ba niŋi dogi n wasi n paali tiŋŋa a.”

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Niŋi Nuhu ni o poga ni ba biduusi ni ba pogiba yi ŋariŋu maa puwa.

text: Lala n na ba nyisi bunvuusira ni niinsi la gba yi ŋariŋu maa puwa, a ni a peerigusi.

text: Naawunni ni wumi bunvuusira la sariga ŋuwa, o yeli o maŋŋa, “M ki lee n si tiŋŋa a noori, a kpali niriba yaniŋisa zu. M baŋiya maa nisaala bibiilim niŋi zaa, fere feera niŋini yabeeri, m ki lee n muri bunvuusira seema ni daa n niŋi la zaa.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Za jemani a wa ti paa durinye nyisigirisim, jemani ni ba niŋi ti n ŋa binaa, bumbutigu ni a naagirisim, waari ni zibiiŋu, wuuni ni seeniŋi, bee ni yiiŋu, a zaa ni mari a jemani.”

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Naawunni nafa Nuhu ni o biisi, ka yeli ba, “Ya dogi biisi, ba wasi n paali tiŋgbaŋaa zaa.

text: Buŋkobiri, niinsi a ni korotosi zaa ni zora ya, ka ba ya nuusi niŋi.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: Pimparaŋa, ya ni dira a zaa, a ni bumbutira, m naani a zaa ti ya, a niŋi ya bindira.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Bunluŋkoso n bi daga ya n di n di niŋi, valiso zeem n bi yi o niŋi, a kpali bo, zeem maa niŋi n na nyevuri be.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: “Ama nyiwune, dogi bidamta, lala jemani ya yaŋsi ni paali tiŋgbaŋaa zaa niŋi.”

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Naawunni yeli Nuhu ni o biisi,

text: a ni binvuusira zaa, niinsi, buŋkobiri a ni bunso zaa n paasi ya niŋi yi ŋariŋu la puwa.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: M naani m sasuwa niŋi sagbana niŋi, wune n ya n niŋi m naŋgbandigiligu maa teesa ti durinye.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Ka sasuwa maa si n yi sagbana maa niŋi, m ni nye u, ka teesi m naŋgbandigiliguso n ka nyisigirisim koo baasigu, a yi m ni ya ni bunvuusira zaa sunsuuniŋi tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi.

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: N ŋa naŋgbandigiligu teesa ŋa n na ma naa n tira bunvuusira zaa.” Nuhu ni o biisi yela

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Nuhu biisi maa n daa n paasi o niŋi yi ŋariŋu maa pu la n ŋa, Sem, Ham ni Jafeti. Ham bii n daa n niŋi Keenan.

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Nuhu biisi bata maa yaŋsi n niŋi nisaaladima zaa n ba tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi la.

text: Nuhu daa n niŋini kokpaara, wune n daa n piili n buri binseesi wala ba naa n maana daam.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ba bayi maa naa waje pili ba bogiri niŋi, a lugi gbinni kpe sugiri maa puwa, a naa waje la pili ba ba, ba da nye o alibana maa.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Daam maa n ti yi Nuhu niŋi, o wumi seema o biisi maa bibila daana n niŋi u,

text: ka yeli, “Naŋgbansiibu niŋini Keenan toori, o ni niŋi guruŋu ti o beya la.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Naawunni n ti Jafeti o wasi, ka ti o yaŋsi ni Sem ba ba niŋi, ama Keenan ni niŋi Jafeti guruŋu.”

ref: and died at the age of
text: O daa dini yuma kobsiwai ni pisinu poin ka kpi.

ref: At first everyone spoke the same language,
text: Piiligu maa, durinye puu niriba zaa daa n togsirini yatogiluŋko.

text: Ba daa n chaŋŋi n kpabira ziya, ba mari wuluŋpusigu, a ti paa paaligu yinni ba boona Babilon paaligu, ka zi nisaaniŋi.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Ba lee n yeli, “Ya cheli ti me tiŋkarigu, ka me kpaa o niŋi, o ba wogilim tisi sagbana, lala jemani o ni ti ti yuuri du, ka ti da pisimi n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.”

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma sigi n ti nyaasi tiŋkarigu la, a ni kpaa maa ba n me la.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Ka yeli, “Pimparaŋa, niribaa niŋini baligu luŋko, a togsira yatogiluŋko, ka piili n niŋi bunso ba n niŋina ŋa. Bo n na ba ni kpabira ba niŋi, ka niŋi u je?

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Ya cheli ti sigi n wa ti jebi ba yatoga maa, lala jemani ba ki lee n wumini taaba yatoga.”

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Tiŋkarigu maa yuuri n daa n niŋi Babilon, a kpali bo, nisaaniŋi Naawunni daa jebi niriba maa yatoga, ka za niŋi pisimi ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Niŋi n na, Tera mari o bii Abram ni o yaŋŋu Loti, Haran biya, o deempoga Sarai, Abram poga, a nyisigi n yi Waa n ba Babilon paaligu, a wara Keenan paaligu. Ba n wara maa, ba wa ti paa seela ba n boona Haran, ka zi niŋi.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Dindaso, Duuma daa yeli Abram, “Yi e paaligaa, e niriba puwa, a ni e ba laanta, a gaa tiŋgbanso ni ya n wusi i ŋa.

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: Ni ti e yaŋsi zoo, ba ni niŋi baliso n mari zaaziya. M ni nafa i, ka ti e yuuri du, e ni niŋi nafa ti niriba.

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: “M ni nafa niseesi ni nafara i, ama ni si niseesi ni siira i noori, e niŋi n na ma ni dolli n nafa balisi zaa.”

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Abram ni o niriba chaŋŋi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi ti paa More titintaa see, a yi Sekem tiŋŋa. A jemani maa Keenan dima daa na ba nisaaniŋi.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma ta Abram saani ti yeli u, “Paaliguso ni naa tira e yaŋsi maa n na la.” Nisaaniŋi Abram me sariga nyisigu daaŋu ti Duuma, so n ta o saani maa.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: A nyaaŋa, o nyisigi n naa o zu to kiŋkagili sazu, a wara tiŋso n zi gunguntiri zu, ka niŋi tiŋkarigu Betel n ba wulunliya, ka Aii mi ba wuluŋpusigu. Ba sabi ba gbana suga, ka me sariga daaŋu, a kpeesi Duuma.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Niŋi n na, o cherigi ziiziisi poli n jina, a naa o zu kpa Keenan kiŋkagili sazu. Abram gaari Yijipiti paaligu

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: Ka Yijipiti dima nye i, ba ni baŋŋi maa m poga n niŋi i. A kpali lala zu, ba ni ku ma, ka cheli i.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Jemanso ba n loo n kpe Yijipiti paaligu, Yijipiti dima maa nye maa o poga maa veela ga.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Poga maa zu, naaba maa mari Abram nene, ka ti u buŋkobiri, peeri, buusi, naagi, buŋsi, gurunsi a ni nyogimasi.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ama, a kpali naaba maa n naa Abram poga Sarai gaari o laanta la zu, Duuma ti toorabeeri gbaa o ni o laanta dima zaa.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Niŋi n na, naaba maa booli Abram, a ti boosi u, “Bo n na e niŋi lala, wula n na e bi yeli ma maa e poga n na?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Wula zu n na e yeli maa e sunzopoga n na, ka ti ma naag u loobi m poga? E poga n na la, mari u kunna.”

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Naaba maa ti o nivusi mari Abram yi paaligu maa niŋi, a ni o poga ni binseesi zaa ba n mari.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: A zu, Abram ni o poga nyisigi Yijipiti paaligu kiŋkagili sagbinni, a pori n kpe Keenan paaligu kiŋkagili sazu, a ni o bindaalim bini zaa, ka Loti gba paag u gaari.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Abram daa niŋini bindaana, a mari peeri, buusi, naagi, gbitisi a ni sugasi.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Niŋi n na, o nyisigi seela o n zi maa, a poli n jili n ti paa Betel ni Aii sunsuuniŋi, seela o n daa daŋŋi n zi la, ka zi niŋi.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: Seela o n daa n me sariga daaŋu ti Duuma la, ka nyisi sariga nisaaniŋi.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Loti, so ninu n daa n chaŋina maa gba, daa marini peeri, buusi, naagi ni o laanta dima

ref: At this time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were still living in the same area, and so there wasn't enough pastureland left for Abram and Lot with all of their animals. Besides this, the men who took care of Abram's animals and the ones who took care of Lot's animals started quarreling.
text: Lala zu, zabiri daa li Abram buŋkokpagiriba ni Loti buŋkokpagiriba niŋi. Lala jemani maa ka Keenan nivusi ni niseesi ba n boona Periziti dima la n daa n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Niŋi n na, Abram yeli Loti, “Ti niŋini dogim luŋko, a bi daga e buŋkokpagiriba ni m buŋkokpagiriba ba zabira,

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Ka tiŋgbaŋŋu maa n do ŋa? Ti barigi taaba. Ka e gaa nuzaa, m ni gaa nudiya, ka e mi gaa nudiya, m ni gaa nuzaa.”

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Loti nyaasi n poli n jili, ka nye maa Joodan falimini, a wa ti paa Zowa, ka niŋi n mari koom nene, a maa Duuma pu la, koo Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu. Ŋama zaa nyaaŋa poin, ka Naawunni daa muri Sodom ni Gomora.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: A zu, Loti nyisi Joodan falimini maa, a kaasi n naag o zu to wuluŋpusigu. Seema a n di niriba bayi maa barigi taaba niŋi n na la.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram zi Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ka Loti mi zi tiŋsi sunsuuniŋi, a yi falimini maa niŋi, a leeli Sodom

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Nisaaniŋi niriba maa daa niŋini ferebeeri dima, ka niŋi yabeeri ga kpe Duuma niŋi. Abram nyisigi n gaari Hebiron

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Loti ni gaa maa nyaaŋa, Duuma yeli Abram, “Za seela e n be ŋa, ka lalli e nufu nyaasi kiŋkagili sagbinni, sazu, a ni wuluŋpusigu ni wulunliya.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: M ya n naani tiŋgbaŋaa, e n nyaasi a ti e ni e yaŋsi, o niŋi ya toori, a ma n wara.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: M ya n ti i ni yaŋdamta, seesi nisaala zaa di n ki tee n ba kaali, ka nira yi tee n kaali tiŋgbaŋŋu a niŋi tanni, lala wune, ba ni tee kaali e yaŋsi gba.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Pimparaŋa, nyisigi n poli n jili tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa, a nyaasi u, a kpali bo, m ya naagi u ni ti i.”

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Niŋi n na, Abram nyisigi seela o n daa zi la, a wa ti zi n begi Mamiri titintaasi, a yi Hebiron, nisaaniŋi n na o me sariga daaŋu ti Duuma.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Sidim falimini maa zaa daa niŋini kootaali boga, jemanso Sodom ni Gomora nadima n zora maa, niriba maa seesi li kootaali boga maa puusi, ka ba seesi zo n du zoori zu.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Ka nadima banaasi maa ti kaasi Sodom ni Gomora dima bunso zaa nyem, haali ba bindira zaa.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Ka ba gbaa Loti, Abram sunzo biya, a ni o bunso zaa nyem gaari.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Daba yindaana, daa n chee n wa ti yeli Abram, Hiburu daba la, bunso zaa n niŋi. A jemani maa Abram daa n zinni leeli daba so ba n boona Mamiri, Amori daba, titintaasi see. Daba maa mabiisi n daa n niŋi Esikol ni Aneri, ba ni Abram daa n mari ni naŋgbanluŋko.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Jemanso Abram n wumi maa ba gbaa o sunzo bii gaari, o laŋsi o laanta puu toozabiriba zaa, ba daa n ba niriba kobsita ni pii ni banii, o mari ba kari nadima banaasi maa bobiri, a wa ti paa ba Dan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Nisaaniŋi n na, o barigi niriba maa gbulagbula, yiiŋu, ba ŋme ba dindima maa niŋi, a nyaŋŋi ba. O kari ba lala haali, a wa ti paa Hooba, Damasika kiŋkagili sagbinni,

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: ba va binseesi zaa ba n naa maa. Ka mari Loti ni o bunso zaa, a ni pogiba ni nivuseesi zaa ba n ma n gaari la pori n ta. Seema Mekizade n nafa Abram n ŋa

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Jemanso Abram n daa n zabi n nyaŋŋi naaba Kedolaama ni o nataaba la, ka pori n ta Sodom naaba tuusi u falimini so ba n boona Sava la niŋi, ka ba boona u naaba falimini.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Ka Mekizade, so n niŋi Salem naaba, ka na kpari n niŋi Duuma Naawunni nakarigu la sandaantumina, o mari paanusi ni daam ti ti Abram,

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: a nafa u yeli, “Naawunni nakarigu la, so n maali saazu ni tiŋŋa la, o nafa Abram.

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: Kpeesigu niŋini Naawunni nakarigu, so n ti e nyaŋŋi e dindima la toori.” Abram nyisi binseesi zaa o ma n yi toowa puu ta la bu piipii zu ti Mekizade.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Sodom naaba yeli Abram, “Mari binseesi zaa e n ma n ta la, ka naa niriba la wune ti ma.”

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Abram yeli u, “M peya, nimbirimootiri, a yi Duuma nufu niŋi, so n niŋi Naawunni nakarigu saazu ni tiŋŋa zaa maana,

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: a maa m ki naa bunso zaa n niŋi e toori, haali borabi koo sabitaa mii gba. E da ti yeli dindaso maa, mani n naa Abram loobi bindaana.”

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: M ki naa sen zaa, ama binseesi m toozabiriba n di la ko. Ama, tori n ti seesi n paasi n niŋi la Aneri, Esikol, a ni Mamiri.

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: N ŋama nyaaŋa, Naawunni nyisi n wusi Abram, ka yeli u, “Da zora dabeem. M ni kogi i yi nimbirimoo zaa niŋi, ka yo i samkarigu.”

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Abram yeli, “Duuma Nakarigu, bo n na e sanyoobu maa ya maali ma, jemanso ni ka biisi ŋa? Eliyeza n niŋi Damasika nira la n si n niŋi m kapete dira.

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: E bi ti ma biisi, m gurunsi ŋa yindaana n ni di m kapete.”

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Niŋi n na, o lee n wumi Duuma ni togsira o saani. “E guruŋu maa ki di e kapete, e gbagba biduwa n ni niŋi e kapete dira”.

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Duuma mari u yi yaŋŋa, ka yeli u, “Nyaasi sagbana zu, ka niŋi aniya kaali ŋmeribiisi a, lala n na e yaŋsi ya n sagi.”

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Abram yeride seema Duuma ni yeli u maa, Duuma deeg u, ka yeli, ni o niŋini yamonsi daana.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli u, “Mani n niŋi Duuma so n pa i yiya, a mari i yi Waa n ba Babilon paaligu, a ti naa tiŋgbaŋaa ti i, o niŋi e buŋ.”

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Abram boosi u, “Duuma Nakarigu, wula ma ya n niŋi baŋŋi maa o niŋini m buŋ?”

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Duuma yeli u, “Mari nagisaa, busaa, pedaa, a zaa a niŋi yuma ataata, a ni kili ni ŋmenni ti ti ma”

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Abram mari lala buŋkobiri maa ti ti Naawunni, ka kasigi a, ayaayi, a digili a bobaayi a tooli taaba, ama niinsi la wune, o bi kasigi a.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Zugusi ta a diibu, Abram kari a basi.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Wuluŋu n ti liira, gbeem gbaa Abram ga, ligu ti pili u birib, dabeem gbaa u.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: E yaŋsi ni paa balisi tulumbunaasi poin, ka pa pori n ta kpe, a kpali Amori dima yabeeri na bi paa tubidarigirisim.”

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Wuluŋu n ti li, zii gbaa, duguso wusi ni yiira o niŋi, a ni miinkpalli ni bugum yi n ti mari buŋkoŋmaasira la sunsuuniŋi gaari.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni ti Abram noori yeli, “M ti i ni noori maa tiŋgbaŋaa, a za Yijipiti torigu, a wa ti paa mogiso ba n boona Yufireeti la zaa ni loobi e yaŋsi toori.”

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Abram poga Sarai daa n bi dogi bii zaa ti u, ama o mari Yijipiti guruŋpoga Heega.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Lala zu, o yeli Abram, “Duuma gbaa m dogim, bo n na e bi mari m guruŋaa doora? Saŋa, o ni dogi bii ti ma.” Abram sagi n pa bunso Sarai n yeli maa.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: A zu, Sarai naa Heega ti u, o loobi o poga. A jemani Abram n daa n wa ti zi Keenan, o yuma pii nyaaŋa n na lala daa n niŋi.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Abram ni Heega lagimi, o mari puwa. Jemanso o n ti baŋŋi maa o mari ni puwa, zunyaabu ta, o bi lee booli Sarai sen.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Niŋi n na, Sarai yeli Abram, “Nyini n ti Heega nyaasira ma jaga. Mani n naagi u ti i, ama o n baŋŋi maa o n mari puu la, o pa nyaasira mani jaga. Naawunni ni di m nini zaa seriya, a baŋŋi so toori n ba niŋi.”

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Abram yeli u, “Nyaasi, e guruŋu n na, nyini n su o kpeeŋu, niŋi u seema zaa e n kpabira.” Sarai namsi u ga, o yi n zo.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Duuma malaaka wa ti paa Heega, a yi bulugu n ba Sora soori, tambisigu tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ka boosi u, “Heega, Sarai guruŋu, ya n na e yi, ka ya n na e wara?” O yeli u, “M zoni wara, ka che m diima.”

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: O yeli u, “Pori n gaa o saani, a wa ti niŋi o guruŋu.”

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Ama, e bii maa ni ba maa buŋŋu n galimi, o ni zabira niriba, ka niriba gba ba zabira u, meesim ki ba o ni o mabiisi puwa.”

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Niŋi n na, Heega boosi o maŋŋa, “Yamonsi n na ma nye Naawunni, ka na ba nyevuri puwa?” Lala zu, o booli Duuma, so n togsi o saani la, Naawunni so n nyaara ma.

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: A zu n na niriba boona bulugu so n do Kadesi ni Bered sunsuuniŋi la, Nyevuri daana so n nyaara ma bulugu.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: M ni digili i noori, ka ti i yaŋdamta.”

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Abram gba, ka zulisi o zu tiŋŋa, Naawunni yeli u.

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: E yuuri bi lee n niŋi Abram, ama Ibrahim, a kpali m loobira i ni balidamta yaadaba.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: M ni ti i yaŋdamta, ba seesi ni niŋi nadima, ka ba seesi ba loobi balisi.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: “M ki tami naŋgbanso ni ti e ni e soosa zu yaŋseesi n ya n ti loobi balisi la, a wara saŋa ni saŋa zaa. M ni niŋi e ni e yaŋsi zaa Naawunni.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Keenan tiŋgbaŋaa zaa, seela e n niŋi saanaa, m ni naag u ti e ni e yaŋsi, o niŋi ba toori saŋa ni saŋa zaa, ka m niŋi ba Naawunni.”

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Naawunni yeli Ibrahim, “E gba daga e sagi n pa m naŋgbandigiligu a, e ni e soosa zu yaŋsi zaa.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Daba zaa yoori ni bi ŋma, lala daana, ba nyisi u yi m niriba puwa, a kpali o bi pa m naŋgbandigiligu maa zu.”

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Naawunni yeli Ibrahim, “A bi daga e n lee n boona e poga yuuri Sarai, za zinaa wara, o yuuri n niŋi Saara.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: M ni nafa u, o dogi biduu ti i, ka m loobi u balidamta ma, nadima ni yi o yaŋsi puwa.”

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ŋama zaa nyaaŋa, o boosi Naawunni, “E ki ti Isimeel o niŋi m kapete dira?”

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Naawunni yeli u, “Akai, e poga Saara ni dogi biduwa, e digili o yuuri Yisaaka. M ni ti naŋgbandigiligu maa ba m ninu sunsuuniŋi, a ni o yaŋsi zaa, a wara. O niŋini naŋgbandigiligu so n ka baasigu.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: M wumi e suusigu, a yi Isimeel pelli, a zu, m ni nafa u, a ti u biisi ni yaŋdamta. Ni naagi u loobi nabiisi pii ni bayi ba, ka m naa o yaŋsi maa baligu loobi nivutintaasi

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Ama m naŋgbandigiligu maa ni pa e bii Yisaaka niŋi, so Saara n ya n dogi yumini jemanaa zu.”

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Jemanso Naawunni n togsi Ibrahim saani baasi, o gaari.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: Daba zaa n daa n ba Ibrahim laanta daa n ŋma ba yoya, a ni o guruŋseesi ba n dogi o laanta, a ni seesi ba n da.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Dindaso wuntonkpaligu, Duuma daa n ta Ibrahim saani, a leeli Mamiri titintaasi. Ka Ibrahim daa n zinni o gbana sugiri noori. Jemanso zii n bii.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: Ka lalli o zu saa, o nyeni dabsi bata n za n leeli u. Noba maa niŋi o n si n nye ba, o chee n we ba saani, a wa ti gba, a naag o gbee zulisi tiŋŋa.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: O suusi ba yeli, “M yidaana jaande, ka m yela peri i, da pa ti saa m yiri.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: M ni ti ba ŋoo koom ti ti ya, ya pegi ya noba, ya zi tiyaa gbinni vuusira, m chena.

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: M ni mari bindira beela gba ta, a ni paasi ya niŋi, ya cheni ŋa puwa. Ya naa mani saa, ya n ta m laanta ŋa.” Ba sagi, ka yeli u, “Niŋi seema e ni yeli maa.”

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Ibrahim zo yomyom kpe o gbana sugiri puwa yeli Saara, “Naag e zumsuma la bowate niŋi paanu yoom.”

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Ka chee n gaari o naagi puu wa ti gbaa nagipolli, so n mari kpaam, a ti ti o guruŋsi yindaana, o ku u, a maali u nene yoom ta.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: O gbagba naa bisimkpaam, nagibisim, a ni valiga ti ti ba tii maa gbinni, ba dira, ka o za ba saani, tii maa gbinni.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Niŋi n na, ba boosi u, “E poga Saara baya?” O yeli ba, ni o ba sugiri puwa.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Ibrahim ni Saara zaa daa n kurigini, haali, Saara daa n bi lee n pegira.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: A la zu, Saara la o maŋŋa, a yi o sufu niŋi, ka yeli, “Pimparaŋa, seela m ni m sira zaa n kurigi n paag a, ni lee n tee n dogi?”

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma boosi Ibrahim, “Wula n na Saara la, ka yeli ni o ki lee n dogi jemanso o n kurigaa?

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Ya bo n ba toom ti Duuma? A maa seema ni yeli la, yumini jemani a zu, m ni lee n pori n ta, ka Saara dogi biduwa.”

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: A kpali Saara n daa zora dabeem zu, o chisi, “M bi la.” O yeli u, “Yamonsi, e laya.” Ibrahim suusi Naawun ti Sodom

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Niŋi n na, niriba maa yi nisaaniŋi wa ti paa seela ba n di n za, ka nye Sodom, ka Ibrahim pa ba zu, o zaali ba soori.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Ka Duuma yeli o sufu niŋi, “M ki naa n sogi Ibrahim bunso ni ya n niŋi.

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: O yaŋsi ni ti niŋi zaaziikara dima ni kpeena baligu, o niŋi n na ma ni dolli n nafa balisi zaa.

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: M nyisi u ni, o wusi o biisi ni o yaŋsi ba mari wumintiibu ti ma, ka niŋini bunso zaa ni tooli, a ni yamonsi. Ka ba yi niŋi lala, m ni niŋi n paali naŋgbanseesi zaa ni digili u la.”

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli Ibrahim, “Yadagiri seesi Sodom ni Gomora dima n niŋina la ti ba yabeeri zoo ga.

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Niŋi n na, dabsi bayi nyisigi n yi ba saani, a naa ba nufu to Sodom soori wara, ama ka Duuma wune na ba Ibrahim saani.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Ibrahim vuu n paa Duuma, ka boosi u, “Yamonsitiri, e ya n murini yasuma ni yabeeri dima zaa?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Yamonsitiri, e ki ku nivusuma ni nivubeeri maa zaa. Lala ki tee n niŋi. Ka e yi niŋi lala, nivusuma la ni nye tubidarigirisim paasi nivubeeri la niŋi. Lala ki tee n niŋi. Durinye ŋa zaa seriya dira daga o niŋi yela ni a soori.”

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Ibrahim lee n togsi, “M Duuma, jaande, che n paŋŋi ma, dabilim so ni kpari n ma n togsira e saani a, nisaala n niŋi ma, m ka soori zaa m tee n yeli i sen.

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Ibrahim yeli u, “Jaande, M Duuma, cheli n togsi e saani. Ka niriba pisiyi ko n ba niŋi?” O yeli u, “M ki muri tiŋŋa maa, ka niriba pisiyi ko ba niŋi.”

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Ibrahim yeli u, “Jaande, da ti e suuri nyisigi, Duuma, m kpabira n togsi bunluŋko paasi. Ka nivusi pii ko ba niŋi?” O yeli u, “M ki muri ba, ka niriba pii ko ba niŋi.”

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: O ni Ibrahim ni togsi n baasi, Duuma gaari, ka Ibrahim pori n gaari o yiri.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Dindaari maa zaanoori, malaakasi bayi la ta Sodom, ka Loti ba n zi tiŋŋa maa voori noori. Noba maa niŋi, o nye ba, ka nyisigi n wa ti tuusi ba. A gba, ka zulisi o zu tiŋŋa.

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: Ka yeli ba, “Ya gaafira, m bani kpe n tumini ya, a zu, ya jaande, ka ta m laanta. Ni ti ya koom, ya pegi ya noba, ka do bee o chee ya nyisigi n wara.” Ba yeli u, “Akai, kulooni ŋa zu n na ti n ya do yiiŋaa.”

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: O suusi ba ga, ba sagi, ka we o laanta, o niŋi bindira ti ba, ba di.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Poin, ka saamba maa ba do, tiŋŋa maa dabsi ti poli n jili laanta maa. Dabsi zaa, bipola ni dabkura.

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: Ba booli Loti tasi n boosi u, “Dabsi la n ta e saani yiiŋaa la ba ya? Mari ba yi n ta, ti mari ba do.”

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Loti yi, ka yo dinoori maa, ka ta ba saani.

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: O yeli ba, “M zodima, m suusira ya ni, ya da pa ti niŋi niribaa, n ŋa yabeegaa.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Ya nyaasi, m mari suŋgurisi bayi, seesi na n bi baŋŋi dabsi. Ya cheli m mari ba yi n ti ya, ya n tee n niŋi ba seema zaa ya n kpabira. Ama, ya da niŋi dabsaa yelli zaa, m saamba n na, ka a daga ni kogi ba.”

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Niŋi n na, ba yeli u, “Maali n za ti nufu niŋi, ka ti ti soori, nyini saana, ŋani n niŋi i, e wusi ti bunso ti n di niŋi? Ti ni namsi i gaa ba.” Ba turi Loti basi nyaaŋa, ka gaa ba wa ti saami dinoori maa.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Dabsi bayi maa ba laanta ka darigi Loti kpe, ka yo dinoori maa.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Ka ba ti niriba maa zaa, nivukura ni bipola, n ba yaŋŋa la zoomi, ba kpabi dinoori maa je. Loti zo n yi Sodom

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Niŋi n na, Loti wa ti yeli o deendima seesi n belimina o bipunsi la, “Ya yi kpe yomyom, Duuma ya n saamini kpe.” Ba nyaasi maa o yomsira ba ni.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Bogufiili, nodakumisi jemani, malaakasi maa yeli Loti, ni ba yi yoom. “Mari e poga ni e bipunsi bayi ŋa yi, lala jemani ya ki koŋŋi ya nyevuya, jemanso tiŋaa n ya n saami a.”

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Loti na mari nagima, ama Duuma zo u nimbaa, lala zu, dabsi bayi maa gbaa o ni o poga ni biisi nuusi darigi ba yi tiŋŋa maa puwa.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Malaakasi maa yindaana yeli ba, “Ya chee, a moligi ya nyevuya. Ya da pa loobi n nyaasi nyaaŋa, ka da pa za nantaŋaa puwa. Ya chee n du zoogaa, lala wune, ya ki kpi.”

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Ya niŋi yatintaa ga ti ma, ya ni moligi n nyevuraa. Ama, zoori maa zu ba wogilim ga, ka m n ya n chee paa nisaaniŋi, daaŋu maa ni gbaa ma, m kpi, ka m na bi paa nisaaniŋi.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Nyaasi tiŋbilaa. Wune n leeli, ka bi zoo. Cheli m du n gaa niŋi. Niŋi n na, ma ni nye moligirisim.”

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: O yeli u, “O veela, m sagiya, m ki saami lala tiŋŋa maa.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Chee yomyom, m ki tee n niŋi bunso zaa, ama, e paani niŋi poin.” A kpali Loti ni booli u tiŋbila la zu, ba boona u Zowa. Sodom ni Gomora mutigu yela

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Wuluŋu daa n si n pusirini, ka Loti paa zowa.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Noba maa niŋi, Duuma ti jiribi ni bugum yi saa ti sigi Sodom ni Gomora niŋi,

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: a di tiŋsi ayi maa ni a tiŋgbana, a ni a niriba ni bumbula zaa nyem,

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Ama, Loti poga loobi n nyaasi, ka loobi yaarim kugiri.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: A n chee bee boguuniŋi, Ibrahim chaŋŋi yoom ta seela o ni Duuma n si n za la,

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: ka nyaasi Sodom ni Gomora a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zaa, o nyeni bugum karigu wusi n duura tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Jemanso Naawunni n daa n muri tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni tiŋsi ayi la, o teesi Ibrahim yela, ka nyisi Loti yi tiŋŋa maa mutigu niŋi, o nye tuligirisim. Loti ni o bipunsi bayi yela

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Bikpeema daana la yeli o sunzo, “Ti ba kurigiya, dabsi mi ka tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi ti n di n kulisi ba, a dogi biisi seema n daga.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Ti ti m ba o kuuli, lala ti ni tee n mari u do, a dolli o niŋi dogi biisi.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: A dindaari yiiŋu, ba ti u daam o ŋu, kpeemdaana la mari o ba do. Ama, o n daa kuuli n gali zu, o bi baŋŋi bunso n niŋi.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Bee n chee, bikpeema la yeli o sunzo, “M mari u do yiiŋu la, ti lee n ti o kuuli yiiŋaa, ka e mi nunu ya do. Lala jemani, ti zaa ni dolli m ba niŋi dogi biisi, ti baligaa o da muri.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: A yiiŋu, ba lee n ŋuusi u, ka bibila daana maa mari u do. Kuuligu aligaaya zu, ŋala gba o bi baŋŋi.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Kpeema daana la dogi biduwa, ka digili o yuuri Mowabi, nivuseesi ba n boona Mowabi dima la yaadaba n na la.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Bibila daana la gba dogi biduwa, ka digili o yuuri Benami. Wune n niŋi Amoni dima yaadaba.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: ka wusi maa Saara niŋini o sunzopoga. Gera naaba, Abimeliki ti ba naa Saara ti ti u.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Pimparaŋa, pori n naag u ti o sira, Naawun tumu n na, ka o suusi Naawunni ti i, lala jemani, e ki kpi. Ama ka e yi bi pori u ti u, m kpaasira e tubiri maa e ni kpi, e ni e niriba zaa.”

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?

text: Wula zu e niŋi lala?”

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.

text: M sunzopoga n na yamonsi, m ba bii n na, ama, ka m ma biya. Ka ma naag u.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: A zu jemanso Naawunni n nyisi ma yi m ba laanta wara paaligu yindaana tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi la, m yeli u ni maa ka ti si n gaa seela zaa o paasi m niŋi yeli maa o sunzodaba n niŋi ma.”

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Niŋi n na, Abimeliki naag o poga pori n ti u, ka ti u peeri, naagi a ni gurunsi.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Naawun tibi u, ka tibi o poga ni guruŋpogiseesi zaa n ba niŋi, lala jemani, ba zaa ni tee n dogi biisi.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Duuma nafa Saara, a maa seema o ti noori la,

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: o mari puwa, a dogi biduu ti Ibrahim jemanso o n kurigi. Ba daa n dogini bii maa jemanso Naawun n daa zaali maa ba n di n dogi u.

ref: Abraham named his son Isaac,
text: Ibrahim digili o yuuri Yisaaka.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Niŋi n na, bii maa n karigi, dindaso ba n ŋma u bisiri, Ibrahim daa n ti ba di sufupeelim bindirigbaliŋu. Ba kari Heega ni Isimeel

text: Saara nye ba, ka yeli Ibrahim, “Ti guruŋpogaa ni o biyaa ba wara. Pogaa biyaa bi daga o barigi e bini, kapete seesi m bii Yisaaka ni ya n ti di.”

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.

text: ka larimi u wa ti zi, ka yeli, “M ki tee n nyaasira m biya, ka o kpiira.” Ka zi niŋi kumina.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim yeli o gurunsi maa, “Ya ni buŋaa, ya ba kpe, ka m ni biyaa ti gaa sunsuwaa niŋi, a wa ti puusi Naawunni, ka ta.”

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Ibrahim naa daari la ziili o bii Yisaaka, ka mi naa bugum ni suwa mari, ba bayi maa naa n wara.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Ibrahim yeli, “M biya, Naawunni ni ti ti sariga pee maa.” Niŋi n na, ba lee n gbaa ba soori wara.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Ba n ti paa seela Naawunni ni wusi Ibrahim la, o maali sariga daaŋu, ka kaasi daari la pa o niŋi, ka gbaag o bii la, a le u pa sariga daaŋu maa niŋi.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Ka voo suwa, o ku o bii maa.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni malaaka goo yi saazu tasi n booli u yeli, “Ibrahim, Ibrahim.” O sagi, “Mani n ŋa.”

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Malaaka maa yeli, “Da pa siisi biyaa, a ti u daaŋu. Pimparaŋa m pa baŋŋi e n zora Naawunni, a ni wumintiibu, a kpali e naag e biluŋko nyisi sariga.”

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Ibrahim n wumi goo maa, o lalli o zu, ka nye pedaa siila n gari tipolli za. O gbaa pee maa nyisi sariga maa, ka che o bii la.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim digili nisaaniŋi yuuri “Duuma ni ti.” Halli zinni, niriba na yetirini, ni Naawunni zoo zu, o ni ti.

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma malaaka goo lee n booli Ibrahim yi saazu buyi daana yeli,

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: Duuma yeli, “A kpali, e n bi zagsi e n di naa e biluŋko nyisi sariga ti ma la zu, m pe m maŋŋa yuuri niŋi maa,

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: nafa n na ma ni nafa i, e yaŋsi ni zoo maa ŋmeribiisi n ba saazu, a maa teeku noori tambisigu. E yaŋsi ni zabi n nyaŋŋi ba dindima.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: A kpali e ni wumi n ti ma la zu, durinye puu balisi zaa ni nye nafa, a yi e yaŋsi puwa.”

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim loobi n we o gurunsi saani, ba zaa gaa Beesiiba, Ibrahim zi nisaaniŋi. Naho yaŋsi yela

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Naho pogibila so ba daa n boona Reema gba daa n dogi Teba ni Gaham ni Tahasi a ni Maaka.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: M niŋini saana yi ya puwa, ya koosi tiŋgbaŋŋu ti ma, o niŋi seela m di n piira m kpiinsi.

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: “Ti yidaana, wumi ti saani. E niŋini nivutimsigu yi ti puwa, nyaasi ti piiba ziisaa, seela n peri i e pi u. Ti yindaana zaa ki moŋŋi i, seela e di n pi e kpiima.”

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: ka yeli ba, “Ka o niŋi ya kiperi maa, ya n ti m pi u seela n peri ma, lala wune ya suusi Zoha bii Efuron ti ma,

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: ni o koosi o zovoori n ba Makipela, ka niŋi o tiŋgbaŋŋu torigu la ti ma. Samiso zaa o n zaali ma, m ni yo u ya nufu niŋi, ka nisaaniŋi o loobi m niriba piibu ziya.”

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
