text: Efuron gba daa n zinni niriba maa puwa, ka togsira Ibrahim saani, ka niriba maa zaa wumina, a yi tiŋŋa maa voori noori, seela ba n ziira.

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: “Akai, m yidaana, wumi m saani, m naa tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni zovoori maa zaa ti i, m naa n ti i niribaa zaa nufu niŋi, e naa n piira e niriba.”

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Niŋi n na, Ibrahim dobi niriba maa zaa tiŋŋa,

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: ka yeli Efuron, a yi niriba maa zaa nufu niŋi, “Ka e yi naa n ti ma, jaande wumi n ti ma, m ni ti i tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ligiri, deeg a, ka m piira m niriba nisaaniŋi.”

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Ŋama nyaaŋa n na, Ibrahim pi o poga Saara zovoori maa n ba tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, a leeli Mamiri Keenan paaligu.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Ibrahim daa n kurigi n kpe kpebilim niŋi, Naawunni mi daa n nafa u soori zaa niŋi.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: O yeli o gurunsi zaa kpeema, so n nyaasira o yiri bunso zaa, ni o naa o nuu niŋi o kpiiligu gbinniŋi.

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: “M kpabirini maa, e pe, a yi Naawunni so n niŋi saazu ni tiŋŋa zaa Duuma la yuuri niŋi, maa e ki naa Keenan paaligu poga ti m bii Yisaaka.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: Ama, e gaa m yiri paaligu, m niriba gbagba puwa, a wa ti naa poga ti ti m bii Yisaaka.”

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Niŋi n na, guruŋu maa boosi u, “Ka poga maa bi kpabira o pa n zu ta kpe, ni tee n mari e bii maa pori n gaa niŋi?”

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Ibrahim yeli u, “Da pa ti ti m bii maa nobiri no nisaaniŋi.

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: M Duuma Naawunni n ba alizaana, so n nyisi ma yi m ba yiri, a yi ti paaligu niŋi, ka togsi m saani, a digili noori ti ma maa, o ni naa kpe tiŋgbaŋaa ti m yaŋsi. O ni ti malaaka pa i yiya, e wa ti naa poga yi nisaaniŋi, a ti ti m biya.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Ka poga maa bi kpabira o pa e zu ta, naŋgbandigiligu maa bi lee n pa i. Ama, da pa mari m bii pori n gaa nisaaniŋi”.

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: A zu, guruŋu maa naa o nuu niŋi Ibrahim kpiiligu gbinniŋi, ka pe, seema o n yeli u maa.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Niŋi n na, guruŋu maa naa o yidaana nyogimasi piiya, ka naa piinisuma baligu zaa paasi n gaari Mesopoteemiya paaligu, Naho yiri.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: O n wa ti paa tiŋŋa maa, o ti nyogimasi maa gba tiŋŋa, bulugu see, zaanoori jemani, jemanso pogiba ni wara kulisi.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Niŋi n na, o suusi Naawunni, “M Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, ti ma zusuŋŋu zinaa, a wusi m yidaana Ibrahim e nivumantiri.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Nyaasi, m zani begi bulugaa, tiŋaa suŋgurisi ni ta n ti ŋoo koom.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: Ti ba so zaa ni ya n yeli maa o ti ma koom n ŋu, o sagi n ti ma, ka o lee n yeli ni o ni ŋoo koom ti m nyogimasi gba, cheli lala poga maa o niŋi so e n nyisi n ti e guruŋu Yisaaka. Ka lala yi niŋi, ni baŋŋi maa nyini n niŋi m yidaana Ibrahim yasuŋŋu yamonsitiri.”

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Guruŋu maa chee n tuusi u, ka suusi u, “Jaande, ti ma koom beela m ŋu.”

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: O tuu koom maa yoom, ka yeli u, “M yidaana, dee n ŋu.”

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: O kpaa koom maa yomyom niŋi buŋkobiri maa koŋuurigu buŋ niŋi, ka lee n chee wa ti ŋoo koom ti kpaa n paasira, a zaa ti ŋu n piri.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Daba maa naa nufu digili tiŋŋa nyaasira u boin, a nye? Naawunni wumi o suusigu maa?

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Nyogimasi maa n ti ŋu n baasi, daba maa naa suga kunde niŋi o ŋoya, ka naa suga bensi ayi niŋi o nusogsi.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: Ka boosi u, “Ŋani bii n niŋi i? Jaande, ka wusi ma, ti ni nye dii yi e ba laanta do?”

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: O yeli u, “M ba n niŋi Betuwel, Naho ni Milika biya.”

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Ŋama nyaaŋa, o lee n yeli, “Ti mari buŋkobiri bindira, a ni a bindoori, a ni saamba dii, ya n di n do.”

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Daba maa n wumi n ŋa, o gba tiŋŋa, a kpeesi Naawunni.

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: Ka yeli, “Kpeesigu niŋini Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni la toori, so n bi zagsi o nivumantiri ni yamonsitiri yi m yidaana Ibrahim niŋi. Ama, ma wune, Duuma pa ma ni yii soori maa puu ta m yidaana niriba yiri.”

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Bipuŋŋu maa chee n gaa o ma laanta, a wa ti yeli yela maa zaa.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Rabeeka sunzodaba Leban n si n nye o ŋoya kunde la ni bensi la n ba o sunzopoga nuusi, ka wumi seema Rabeeka n yeli bunso daba maa n yeli u, o yi yoom gaa daba maa saani, a wa ti nye o n za n begi o nyogimasi, a yi kuliga maa noori.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: O yeli u, “Chena, nyini so Duuma n nafa, bo n na e za yaŋŋa? M maali dii zaali i, e nyogimasi ŋa gba zii ba niŋi.”

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Niŋi n na, daba maa gaa laanta, ka Leban lerigi nyogimasi la ziiri, ka naa kaga ni bindira ti ti a, ka ŋoo n ti ti daba maa ni niseesi n pa o zu la, ba pegi ba noba.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ba mari bindira ti ti u, ni o di, o yeli, “M ki di, ama m nyisini m soori baaru.” Leban yeli u, “Togsima.”

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Niŋi o yeli, “Ibrahim guruŋu n niŋi ma.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Duuma nafa m yidaana ga, o loobi bindaana, o ti u peeri, buusi, naagi, gbitisi, sugasi, gurunsi dabsi ni pogiba, nyogimasi, a ni buŋsi.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: M yidaana poga Saara, nivukuritiri jemani n na o dogi biduu ti Ibrahim, ka o naag o bunso zaa ti bii maa.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Pimparaŋa, m yidaana ti ma pe maa, ni m da pa ti naa poga yi Keenan paaligu, seela o n zi ŋa, a ti o bii maa.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Ama m gaari o ba yiri, o dogim puwa, o niriba gbagba saani, a naa poga ti ti o biya.

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: “Niŋi n na, ma boosi m yidaana, ka poga maa yeli o ki pa m zu ta?

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: O yeli ma, ni Duuma so o n wumi n tira la ni tumi malaaka o paa ma gaari, bunso zaa, a maali, m naa poga yi o niriba puwa, a yi o ba laanta ti ti o biya.

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: Ni ka m yi gaari o niriba saani, peebu maa ki lee n gbaa ma, halli ka ba moŋŋi ma poga maa, naŋgbansamini maa bi lee n pa ma.

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: “Jemanso m ni paa kuligaa puu zinaa, ma yeli, ‘M Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, ka a niŋi e kiperi, jaande, ti m nye zusuŋŋu, m cheni ŋa puwa.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: Nyaasi, m zani kuligaa puu n na la, ka suŋguri zaa taya, o ti ŋoo koom, ka ma yeli u, “Jaande, ti ma e koom maa beela m ŋu.”

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: Ka o yeli ma, “Ŋuma, ka m ŋoo n ti e nyogimasi ŋa gba,” cheli o niŋi so Duuma n nyisi n ti m yidaana Ibrahim biya.’

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: “M na bi baasi Naawunni suusigu maa m sufu niŋi, ka Rabeeka bugi o koom yuuri ta, a sigi kuliga ŋoo koom, ma yeli u, ‘Jaande, ka ti ma koom beela m ŋu.’

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: “O tuu o koom yuuri maa yomyom yi o bogu niŋi, ka yeli ma, ‘Ŋu ma, m ni ŋoo n ti e nyogimasi ŋa gba.’ A zu, ma ŋu, ka o ti nyogimasi maa gba.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: “Ma boosi u, ‘Ŋani bii n niŋi i?’ O yeli, ‘M ba n niŋi Betuwel, Naho ni Milika biya.’ Niŋi n na, ma naa kunde niŋi o ŋoya, ka naa bensi niŋi o nusogsi.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Ma gba tiŋŋa puusi Duuma, m yidaana Ibrahim Naawunni, so n pa ma yiya, a ni seema n daga, ma nye m yidaana Ibrahim mabii yaŋŋu ti o biya.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: “A zu, ya yeli ma, ka ya ni wusi m yidaana nivumantiri ni yamonsitiri, ya ti m baŋŋi, ka ya mi ki lee ti, ya yeli ma, m baŋŋi, soso m di n dolli.”

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Niŋi n na, Leban ni Betuwel yeli, “Yela ŋa zaa yini Duuma saani, a zu, ti wune bi lee n mari sen ti n di n yeli.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Rabeeka n ŋa, mari u wa ti ti e yidaana bii maa, o niŋi o poga, a maa seema Duuma ni zaali la.”

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Niŋi n na, o nyisi sugasi ni gbitisi pogisara bini, a ni bimpinna ti Rabeeka, ka ti o sunzodaba ni o ma piiniseesi n mari ligiri ga.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: O n niŋi n ŋa baasi, o ni niseesi n daa n na la di, ka ŋu, ka do niŋi. A n kaa bee boguuniŋi, Ibrahim guruŋu maa suusi soori, “Ya ti ma soori, m gaa m yidaana saani.”

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka sunzodaba ni o ma yeli, “Cheli Rabeeka o na ba kpe dapii poin, ka ya mari u wara.”

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: O yeli ba, “Ya da laŋŋi ma nyaaŋa. Duuma ti ma ni zusuŋŋu, a yi m cheni ŋa puwa, a zu, ya basi ma soori m gaa m yidaana saani.”

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Niŋi n na, ba yeli, “Cheli ti booli bipuŋŋu maa gbagba boosi u.”

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Ba booli Rabeeka boosi u, “E ni paa dabaa gaari? O sagi Mm, m ni gaa.”

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: A zu, ba sagi, ka ti Rabeeka ni o bipoga paa Ibrahim guruŋu la ni o niriba maa gaari.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Ba suusi Naawunni ti Rabeeka ka yeli, “Sunzopoga, Naawunni n nafa i, ka ti e biisi wasi tusatusa, ka e dogim puu dima zaa ni nyaŋŋi ba dindima, a ni ba tiŋsi.”

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka ni pogiseesi n pa o zu niŋi siriya du ba nyogimasi, a paa daba maa gaari.

ref: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Yisaaka daa n yini buluguso ba n boona nyevuri daana bulugu so n nyaara ma, tambisigu tiirigu, a wa ti ba Keenan kiŋkagili sazu Negef.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Yisaaka mari Rabeeka kpe o ma gbana sugiri puwa, a naag u loobi o poga, ka daa n kpabira u ga. O ma kum sufusaaŋu n daa mari u, sufupeelim pa ta.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: O dogi Zimaran, Jokasina, Medan, Midiyan Isibaka a ni Suwa.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Jokasina dogi Seba ni Dedan. Dedan biduusi n daa n niŋi Asurum dima, Letusi dima, a ni Lumiti dima.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Ama, jemanso o n daa n na ba o nyevuri niŋi, o daa n pini o poga seesi biduusi piini. Ka nyisi n ŋa biduusi a gaari tiŋŋa yindaana n ba wuluŋpusigu, ka che o bii Yisaaka. Ibrahim kum yela

ref: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him east of Hebron in Machpelah Cave that was part of the field Abraham had bought from Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite. Abraham was buried there beside his wife Sarah.
text: O biisi, Yisaaka ni Isimeel, pi u yi Makipela zovoori niŋi, a leeli Mamiri. Tiŋgbanso Ibrahim n daa n da, Zoha bii Efuron n niŋi Hititi nira la saani, niŋi n na ba pi o ni o poga Saara.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Ibrahim kum nyaaŋa, Naawunni nafa o biduu Yisaaka, ka o zi n leeli buluguso ba n boona nyevuri daana bulugu, so n nyaara ma.

ref: Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the slave woman of Sarah.
text: Isimeel biisi yela Isimeel, so Heega, Saara Yijipiti guruŋbipoga n dogi ti Ibrahim, biisi yela n ŋa.

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Isimeel biduusi yuya, a ni seema ba n pa taaba zu. Naboti, Keda, Adibeel, Mibisam.

ref: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text: Misihama, Duma, Masa.

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: Hadadi, Tema, Jetu, Nafisi a ni Kedema.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: Barima n daa niŋi pii ni ayi la, ka ba naa ba yuya digili ba tiŋsi ni seelasi ba n zi.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Isimeel baligu maa daa zinni Havila ni Suu sunsuuniŋi, Yijipiti wuluŋpusigu, Asiriya soori. Ba ni ba niriba daa n bi lagimi n zi. Isawu ni Yakubu dogim yela

ref: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
text: Seema Ibrahim bii Yisaaka ni o baligu n piili n ŋa.

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: A kpali Rabeeka n daa n na bi dogi zu, Yisaaka suusi Naawunni. Duuma wumi o suusigu, Rabeeka mari puwa.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Jaasi puu n na Rabeeka daa n mari, biisaa mugisira taaba ba ma puu niŋi. O boosi Naawunni, “Wula mini n na ŋa?”

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli u, “Balisi bayi n ba e puu niŋi, ba ni ti zabira taaba ŋuuri. Yinni ni mari kpeeŋu gaari o to, ka kpeema daana ni tumina bibila daana la.”

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Yipaara maa daa n niŋini nivuzee, ka o niŋa zaa mari koba, a maa o ni pili gbaŋŋu tagite. Lala zu, ba digili o yuuri Isawu.

ref: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
text: O n yiira la, o sunzo gbaa o nosiŋkpaari mari. Lala zu ba digili o yuuri Yakubu. Jemanso Rabeeka ni dogi biisaa, ka Yisaaka niŋini yuma pisiyoobu. Isawu koosi o wurikoŋtiri

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Biisaa n ti karigi, Isawu loobi toosa, a baŋŋi toosilim ga, ka kpabira moo puu chaŋinisim. Ka Yakubu ba boin, ka kpabira yiri ga.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Yisaaka kpabira Isawu yela, a kpali o n kuura valiga o ŋobira la zu, ka Rabeeka mi kpabira Yakubu yela.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Dindaso Yakubu daa dugira ziiri, ka Isawu yi moo puu ta, a ni kom.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: A zu, Isawu yeli Yakubu, “Ti ma e zimooma maa seesi, kom mari ma ga.” Lala zu n na ba boona u Idom la.

ref: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
text: Yakubu yeli u, “Naa e dogim kpebilim ŋa ti ma poin, ka m mi ti i ziiraa.”

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Isawu yeli u, “Nyaasi, kom n ya n ku ma, bo tono n na kpebilim mari?”

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Yakubu yeli u, “Pe.” Isawu sagi, ka pe, a naa o kpebilim maa tagi ziiri.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu ti u paanu ni tuya ziiri, o ŋobi, ka pebi ziiri maa, ka nyisigi n gaari. Seema Isawu n daa n zagsi o dogim kpebilim n na la.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Jemanso, kom daa n lini paaligu maa, pa kom so n daa n li Ibrahim jemani la, ŋala daa n kurini. Kom maa jemani n na Yisaaka nyisigi n we Filisiti naaba Abimeliki saani yi Gera.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma nyisi o maŋŋa wusi Yisaaka, ka yeli u n ŋa, “Da pa gaa Yijipiti paaligu, ba tiŋgbanso ni yetira i ŋa niŋi.

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Ba kpe paaligu daba ayi, m ni ba e saani, ka nafa i. M ya n pa ni m naŋgbantiibu so ni ti e ba Ibrahim la, m ni naa tiŋgbaŋaa zaa ti e ni e yaŋsi.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: M ni ti e yaŋsi wasi, a zoo maa ŋmeribiisi, m ni kpabira ba yela, ka dolli ba niŋi nafa durinye balisi zaa.

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: A zu, Yisaaka zi Gera

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Rabeeka daa n niŋini pogiso n veela ga, nisaaniŋi dabsi daa n boosira u o yela, o yeli ni o sunzopoga n na. O daa n zorini dabeem, o n di n yeli ba ni o poga n na, ba n di n ku, ka naa poga maa.

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Yisaaka zi nisaaniŋi yuu. Dindaso Abimeliki Filisiti naaba nyaasi o tokuro niŋi, ka nye Yisaaka ni gbaa o poga Rabeeka kpabirisimtiri.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Naaba Abimeliki booli Yisaaka, a boosi u, “E poga n niŋi pogaa? Ka wula zu n na, e yeli e sunzopoga n niŋi u?” Yisaaka yeli u, “M feni maa ba ni ku ma kpali o zu.”

ref: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
text: “Bo n na e niŋi ti seema? Ka dabsaa yinni zaa di mari e pogaa do, ti di niŋi yabetintaa.”

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: A zu, naaba Abimeliki kpaasi so zaa tuba, ni so zaa ni siisi dabaa ni o pogaa, ni koŋŋi o nyevuri. Yisaaka loobi bindaana

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: O daa n loobini bindaana, ka o bini na si n paasira.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: O daa marini naagi, peeri, a ni gurunsi. Binaa zu, o yela daa n marini Filisiti dima pubeerim.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: A zu, ba gbi tana nyetira buluseesi o ba Ibrahim n daa n ti o gurunsi gbi la zaa.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Dindaso, naaba Abimeliki yeli Yisaaka, “Yi ti puwa, a kpali bo, e kpeeŋu nyaŋŋi ti.”

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: O n gaa n niŋi maa, o lee maali n yaa buluseesi ba n daa n gbi Ibrahim jemani la, ka Filisiti dima daa nyeri a, Ibrahim kum nyaaŋa la, Yisaaka lee n naa yuseesi o ba n daa n digili a la gbagba n digili a.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Dindaso, Yisaaka gurunsi gbi bulugu yi nantaŋŋu maa puwa, ka nye koom.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: A daa n bi yuu, ka Gera buŋkokpagiriba ni Yisaaka buŋkokpagiriba daa zabira ni, bulugu maa niŋini ba toori. A zu, Yisaaka digili lala bulugu maa yuuri Zabiri.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka gurunsi maa lee n gbi bulugu, ba ni Gera buŋkokpagiriba maa lee n mari o naŋgbanchee, Yisaaka digili o yuuri Diŋŋu.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: A kpali ba naŋgbanchee maa zu, Yisaaka nyisigi nisaaniŋi, a wa ti zi zipaali, ka lee n gbi bulupaali. Nisaaniŋi wune, naŋgbanchee daa n lee n ka niŋi, a zu, o digili niŋi yuuri niŋgbirimaasim.

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Ŋama nyaaŋa, o lee n nyisigi n gaa Beesiiba.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: Dindaari maa yiiŋu, Duuma nyisi o maŋŋa wusi u, ka yeli u, “Mani n niŋi e ba Ibrahim Naawunni la, a zu, da zora dabeem, m ni ba e saani, ka nafa i, ka ti e yaŋsi zoo, a kpali m guruŋu Ibrahim zu.”

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka me sariga daaŋu, ka kpeesi Duuma, ka sabi o gbana suga.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
text: Dindaso, Abimeliki ni Ahuza, o jinatoora, a ni Fikoli o soojasi kpeema yi Gera ta Yisaaka saani.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka boosi ba, “Bo n na ya chena m saani ya ti niŋi? Ni daa n ba ya saani, ya daa n chisi ma ni, ka kari ma yi ya saani.”

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Ba yeli u, “Ti nyeya maa Duuma bani e saani, a zu, ti ta, ti niŋi naŋgbanluŋko, a pe, ka digili noori ti taaba,

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: a maa e ki niŋi ti daaŋu zaa, seema ti gba ni daa n bi namsi i, jemanso e n daa n ba ti saani la, ka yi ni niŋgbirimaasim. Ti baŋiya maa, Duuma nafa i ni.”

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka dugi bindira ga, ba di, ka ŋu.

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: A n chee bee bogufiili, ba zaa nyisigi n digili noori ti taaba, ka Yisaaka beeli ba soori, ba mari sufupeelim kuli.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Dindaari maa gbagba, Yisaaka gurunsi ta o saani ti yeli u, seema ba n gbi bulugu, a paa koom n ŋa.

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Yisaaka daa digili bulugu maa yuuri Siiba, o gbinni n niŋi naŋgbandigiligu. Seema a n di, ba boona tiŋŋa maa Beesiiba n na la, halli zinni. Isawu pogiba yela

ref: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Pogiba bayi maa daa ti ni beebu loobi toom ti Yisaaka ni o poga Rabeeka.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Yisaaka n daa n ti kurigi, ka bi lee n nyaara nene, dindaso, o booli o bikpeema daana Isawu, “M biya.” O sagi “Mani n ŋa.”

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka yeli u, “Pimparaŋa m kurigiya, ka bi baŋŋi jemanso m di n kpi.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Nyisigi n naa e logu ni o piima gaa moo niŋi, a wa ti ku valiga ti ti ma.

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: Ka e mari n ŋa valigaa ta, e naag u dugi bindira meesa ti ti ma, m di, ka nafa i poin, ka kpi.”

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Niŋi Rabeeka yeli o bii Yakubu, “Nyaasi, m wumi e ba n yetira e beeri Isawu,

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: ni o wa ti ku valiga ti dugi bindira meesa ti o di, ka nafa u poin, ka kpi.

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: A zu, m biya, yoo tuba nene wumi, ka niŋi seema ni ya n yeli i ŋa.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Yi dinyaaŋa we buŋkobiri la puwa, a wa ti gbaa bupola ayi ta, m dugi bindira meesa ti e ba,

ref: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
text: e naa bindira maa wa ti ti e ba o di, ka nafa i poin, ka kpi.”

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli o ma Rabeeka, “Ka m beeri niŋa zaa ni mari koba, ka m mi niŋa n ka koba ŋa.

ref: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
text: Ka m ba siisi ma, o ni yeli mani n yomsira u, ka ki nafa ma, ama o ni si ma noori.”

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: O ma yeli u, “Cheli naŋgbansiibu maa o niŋi m toori, nyiwune e niŋi seema ni yeli i maa de, wa ti gbaag a ti ti ma.”

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu wa ti gbaa buusi maa ti ti o ma, o dugi bindira meesa, seema gbagba Yisaaka ni kpabira la.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Ka naa o bii Isawu, so n niŋi kpeema la, bimpilisuma n ba dii pili o sunzo Yakubu.

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: Ka lee n naa buusi la gbana muuri Yakubu nuusi ni o nuŋgori, seelasi ni ka koba la.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: O n pili u n ŋa baasi, o naa bindira maa ni paanu so o ni se la paasi.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Niŋi n na, o dee bindira la gaa o ba saani, ka yeli, “M ba.” O ba sagi, m biya, “Wunbo n na?”

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Yakubu yeli o ba, “Mani Isawu, e bikpeema n na. M niŋi seema e ni yeli ma ni n niŋi la, a zu, nyisigi n zi, a di bindira maa, ka nafa ma.”

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Yisaaka boosi u, “M biya, wula n na e nye e valigaa yoom seema?” O yeli, “Duuma so n niŋi e Naawunni la n ti ma zusuŋŋu.”

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Niŋi n na, Yisaaka yeli u, “Vuu n ta m saani, m siisi i nyee, m bii Isawu gbagba n na ŋa, koo ka wune n na?”

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Yakubu vuu n we o ba saani, o siisi u, ka yeli, “E goo niŋini Yakubu goo, ka e nuusaa mi niŋi Isawu nuusi.”

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Ama, poin ka o nafa u maa, o boosi u ni, “M bii Isawu gbagba n ŋa?” O sagi, “Mm.”

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: A zu, Yisaaka yeli u, “Naa moo puu valiga ni bindira maa ta, m di, ka nafa i.” Yakubu naa bindira maa ti ti u, o di, ka naa daseesi gba ti u, o ŋu.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: Niŋi n na, o ba Yisaaka yeli u, “M biya, vuu n ti mugisi ma.”

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Yakubu vuu n wa ti mugisi u, Yisaaka ni wumi o bimpinna maa ŋuwa, o nafa u, ka yeli n ŋa, “Ee, m biyaa si n ŋusirini maa tiŋgbaŋaa Duuma ni nafa.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Naawunni n ti i saazu magilim, a ti i pubindira ga, halli chi baligu ni buŋŋuura zaa.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Naawunni o ti balisi ba tumina i, ka durinye dima ba dobira i tiŋŋa. E ni niŋi yidaandaba yi e mabiisi niŋi. Ka niseesi ni si i noori ni nye naŋgbansiibu, ka seesi ni nafa i, ni nye nafa.” Isawu suusi nafa yi Yisaaka saani

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Yisaaka ni si n nafa Yakubu ti baasira, ka o beeri Isawu yi o moo puu goorim ta.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Niŋi n na, o gba dugi bindira meesa ti ti o ba, ka yeli u, “M ba, nyisigi n zi, a di m bindira, ka nafa ma.”

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: O ba Yisaaka boosi u, “Ŋani n na?” Bii maa yeli, “Mani e bikpeema Isawu.”

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: A ku Yisaaka noori, o niŋa dammi u, o boosi, “Ka ŋani mini n go moo, a ku valiga dugi n ti ti ma? M si n dini baasi, ka nafa u, beela ka e ta la. Yamonsi, o ni nye nafa.”

ref: Esau cried out in great distress, “Father, give me a blessing too!”
text: Isawu n wumi seema o ba n yeli maa, o chaari kpeeŋtiri ni sufusaaŋu, ka yeli o ba, “Nafa m gba, m ba.”

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: “E sunzo tee n ti yomsi ma, a deeg e nafa maa.”

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Niŋi n na, Isawu yeli o ba, “O buyi daana n na la, o n puni ma, a jaa, a zu, o yuuri di Yakubu. O daa n dee m dogim kpebilim, ka lee n dee m nafa.” Ka o lee n boosi o ba, “Ama, e bi che nafa maa yinni zaali ma?”

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Yisaaka yeli o bii Isawu, “M tee n naag u loobi e yidaana, ka naag o mabiisi zaa loobi o nachiinsi. O ni nye chi baligu ga, a ni daam ga. Wula n na ma lee n ya niŋi i, m biya?”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Niŋi n na, Isawu boosi o ba, “M ba, nafaluŋko n na e mari? Nafa m gba.” Ka chaari kpeeŋtiri.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: O ba Yisaaka yeli u, “E ziiziya ni tiŋgbansuŋŋu ni ba wogilim, magilim ki yi saazu ti maag e tiŋgbaŋŋu.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: E tokobi niŋi n na, e ni dolli n dira, ka e ni niŋi e sunzo nachiima. Ama, dindaso zaa e ni zagsi suurisi maa, e ni fa e zu yi o nuusi niŋi.” Yakubu zo n gaa Leban saani

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Niŋi n na, Isawu chisi Yakubu, a kpali nafa la o ba n nafa u la zu, ka fe n ŋa yi o sufu niŋi. “A ki lee n yuu, ka m ba o kpi, ti kumi o kuuri, kuuri maa nyaaŋa n na ma ni ku m sunzo Yakubu.”

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Rabeeka ni baŋŋi seema Isawu ni le o sufu niŋi, o tumi n booli o bibila daana Yakubu, a yeli u, “E beeri Isawu ya n ku i ni, a yo samini.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: A zu, biya, wumi nene, ka niŋi seema ni ya n yeli i ŋa. Yi kpe yoom, a zo n gaa m sunzodaba Leban saani, a yi Haran tiŋŋa.

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Ka ba o saani daba ayi, e beeri suuri o maa poin.

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: Ka o suuri ti maa, m ni tolimi booli i. A kpali, m bi kpabira n koŋŋi ya bayi zaa dindaluŋko.”

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Niŋi n na, Rabeeka yeli o sira Yisaaka, “M sufu bi maa ma, a kpali, Hititi pogiba la zu. Ka Yakubu gba lee n naa Hititi poga, m di kpiya so.”

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Yisaaka booli Yakubu ti nafa u, ka yeli u, “Da pa ti naa Keenan paaligu poga.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Ama, gaa Mesopoteemiya paaligu, a wa ti kpe e yaadaba Betuwel laanta. Naa poga yi nisaaniŋi, a yi e ma sunzodaba Leban bipunsi puwa.

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Naawunni, kpeeŋu zaa daana, o nafa e kulisigu, a ti ya bidamta. Lala jemani, e ni ti loobi balidamta ba.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Wune n ni nafa e ni e yaŋsi maa seema o n daa n nafa Ibrahim la, a ti tiŋgbaŋaa loobi e toori, so niŋi e ni be ŋa, wune Naawunni n daa naa n ti Ibrahim la.”

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: A zu, Yisaaka ti Yakubu gaari Mesopoteemiya, Leban saani. Leban ba n niŋi Betuwel, so n niŋi Aram nira, ka niŋi Yakubu ni Isawu ma Rabeeka sunzodaba.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Isawu lee n naa poga paasi Isawu wumi Yisaaka ni nafa Yakubu, ka ti o gaa Mesopoteemiya, o wa ti naa poga nisaaniŋi, ka lee n wumi maa o nafa u, ka yeli u, “Da pa ti naa Keenan paaligu poga.”

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: Yakubu wumi o ba ni o ma noori ka gaa Mesopoteemiya.

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: Isawu baŋŋi maa Keenan paaligu pogiba maa yela bi peri o ba Yisaaka.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: A zu, o gaa Ibrahim bii Isimeel saani wa ti naa o bipuŋŋu Mahalaf, Nebayof sunzopoga, a paasi o pogiseesi o mari la niŋi. Yakubu zaasim yela a yi Betel

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Yakubu yi Beesiiba wara Haran tiŋŋa.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: O n paa seela, o do niŋi bee o chee, a kpali wuluŋu liya. Ka naa kugiri loobi zukogu kogi n do.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: O zaasi zaasim, a nye darigu n za tiŋŋa wa ti tisi sagbana, ka Naawunni malaakasi duura o niŋi, ka sigira.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Ka Duuma za n begi darigu maa yi saazu, ka yeli, “Mani n niŋi Duuma la, Ibrahim Naawunni, a ni Yisaaka Naawunni. Tiŋgbaŋŋu so niŋi e ni do ŋa, m ni naag u ti e ni e yaŋsi.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: E yaŋsi ni sagi maa tambisigu yi durinye ŋa puwa, ya ni saligi n gaa wulunliya, wuluŋpusigu, kiŋkagili sagbinni, a ni kiŋkagili sazu. M ni nafa balisi zaa yi durinye ŋa puwa, a dolli e ni e yaŋsi niŋi.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: Teesira, m ni ba e saani, a kogira i seela zaa e n wara, m ni mari i pori n ta tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi. M ki cheli i, ama bunso zaa ni digili noori yeli, m ni niŋi maa niŋini.”

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Jemanso Yakubu ni nyisigi gbeem maa niŋi, o yeli, “Yamonsitiri, Duuma ba kpe, ka ma bi baŋŋi, o lala.”

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Dabeem gbaag u, o yeli, “Kpe yela mari dabeem gbagba. Ka buŋ zaa n lee na n paasi Naawunni dii ni Alizaana dinaŋgbanni.”

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: A n chee bee bogufiili, Yakubu naa kugiri la o n kogi la damsi n sabi, ka kpaa kpaam pa o niŋi, a naag u pobi Naawunni nuusi.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: Ka digili nisaaniŋi Betel. Tiŋŋa maa yuuri n daa n niŋi Luzi.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Yakubu digili noori yeli, “Naawunni, ka e yi ba m saani, a kogira ma yi n cheni ŋa puwa, ka tira ma bindira ni bimpinna,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: ma lee n ti pori n gaari m ba yiri ni alaanfiya, nyini n ni niŋi m Naawunni.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Teesigu kugiraa ni sabi seela ŋa, ni niŋi e kpeesigu ziya, ka bunso zaa e n ti ma, m ni nyisi a piipii zu ti i.”

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Yakubu lee n gbaa soori ti paa niseesi n ba wuluŋpusigu tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Niŋi n na, o nye bulugu, ka peeri tulum buta zu do o see, a kpali, bulugu maa n na ba ŋoo n niŋini peeri maa. Kugiyeliŋu n daa pa bulugu maa noori.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Peeri maa zaa yi ti laŋsi, pekpagiriba maa yi bilimini kugiri la, ka ŋoo koom niŋi peeri maa. Ba yi lee n porini kugiri maa pa bulugu maa noori.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Yakubu boosi pekpagiriba maa, “M zodima, ya n na ya yi?” Ba yeli u, “Ti yini Haran.”

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: O lee n boosi ba, “Ya baŋŋi Naho yaŋŋu Leban?” Ba sagi, “Mm, ti baŋŋi u.”

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Yakubu boosi ba, “O mari alaanfiya?” Ba yeli u, “O mari alaanfiya. Nyaasi, o bipuŋŋu Rachel ni o peeri n chena la.”

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: O yeli ba, “Ya nyaasi, wuluŋu na zooya ga, jemani na bi paa maa peeri maa zaa di n laŋsi. Ya ŋoo koom niŋi a, ka mari a pori n gaa diibu ziya.”

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Ba yeli u, “Ti ki tee, ama peeri maa zaa laŋsini poin, ka ti tee n bilimi kugiri maa yi bulugu maa noori, ka ŋoo koom niŋi a.”

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Jemanso Yakubu na n togsira ba saani maa, ka Rachel mari o ba peeri ta, wune n daa kpagiri a.

ref: When Jacob saw her and his uncle's sheep, he rolled the rock away and watered the sheep.
text: Yakubu n nye Rachel ni o nyesiba Leban peeri, o gaa bulugu maa see bilimi kugiri la, ka ŋoo koom niŋi o nyesiba peeri maa.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu mugisi Rachel, ka kumi, a ni sufupeelimtiri.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Ka yeli Rachel maa, o niŋini o ba sunzopoga Rabeeka biya. Rachel chee yoom wa ti yeli o ba.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Leban ni si n wumi Rabeeka biduu Yakubu baaru, o zo yoom ti tuusi u. A ŋaami u tabi o nyaari, ka mugisi u, ka mari u ta laanta, Yakubu yeli u bunso zaa n niŋi.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Leban yeli u, e niŋini m valiga ni m zeem, Yakubu ba o saani ŋmerigunni. Yakubu tumi Leban Rachel ni Liya zu

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Leban boosi u, “A kpali e nira n niŋi ma zu, e tumi n ti ma jaga? Yeli ma e sanyoori.”

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Liya nimbiisi daa n bi niŋi dede, ama Rachel wune daa n veela pogisara ga.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Yakubu daa n kpabira Rachel, a zu, o yeli u, m ni tumi n ti i yuma ayapoin, ka e naa e bibila daana Rachel ti ma.

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Leban yeli u, “M ni naag u ti i n so, m ni naag u ti dabyindaana. Zi n saani kpe.”

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Yakubu tumi yuma ayapoin, a kpali Rachel zu, ka a ŋmaani maa daasi beela yi o saani, seema o n kpabira Rachel la zu.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Dindaso, Yakubu yeli Leban, “M tuma la jemani baasiya, cheli m ni e bii Rachel kulisi.”

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: A zu, Leban laŋsi tiŋŋa maa niriba zaa, a ti ba dugi poginaagirisim bindira ti ba.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Ama yiiŋu n zibi, o naa Liya ti Yakubu, o mari u do.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Bee n ti chee Yakubu baŋŋi maa Liya n na. O wa ti boosi Leban, “Bo n na e niŋi ma lala? M tumi i ni, ka e naa Rachel ti ma, wula zu n na e yomsi ma?”

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Leban yeli u, “Ti kpe, ti kaari chisirini bii ni daŋŋi kpeema kulisi.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Baasi e pogipaali yaniŋisa zaa daba ayapoin nyaaŋa, ka m naa Rachel ti i, ka e lee n tumi n ti ma yuma ayapoin.”

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Yakubu mari Rachel gba do, ka kpabira u gaa Liya. Niŋi n na, o lee n tumi yuma ayapoin ti Leban.

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Duuma nye maa, o bi kpabira Liya maa Rachel, o yoog o dogim, ka Rachel niŋi poginyee.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Liya mari puwa, a dogi biduwa, ka yeli, “Duuma nye m nimbaa, pimparaŋa m sira ni kpabira ma.” A zu, o digili o bii maa yuuri Rubin.

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: O lee n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa, ka yeli, “Duuma na ti ma ni biduwaa, a kpali o wumiya maa m ka kpabira.” A zu, o digili o yuuri Simiyon.

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: O lee n maali n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa. Ka yeli, “Pimparaŋa m sira ni vuu n kpari ma, a kpali m dogini biduusi bata ti u.” A zu, o digili o yuuri Livai.

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Rachel nye maa o bi dogi bii zaa ti Yakubu, o beeri yela gbaag u pubeerim. A zu, o yeli Yakubu, “Ti ma biisi, ka pa lala, ni kpi.”

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Yakubu suuri nyisigi, o boosi u, “Mani n niŋi Naawunni, a gbaag e dogim mari?”

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Rachel yeli u, “M bipoga Biliha n na la, mari u do, o ni dogi biisi ti ma. O niŋi n na mani dolli n loobi biisi ma.”

ref: and they had a son.
text: O mari puwa, a dogi biduu ti Yakubu.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Rachel yeli, “Naawunni zabi n pa m zu. O wumi m suusigu, ka ti ma biduwa.” Ka digili o yuuri Dan.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Biliha lee n mari puwa, a dogi biduwa paasi n ti Yakubu.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Rachel yeli, “M zabi m beeri kpeeŋtiri, ka mi nyaŋŋi.” A zu, o digili bii maa yuuri Nafitali.

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Liya n ti nye maa o bi lee dogira, o naa o bipoga Zilipa ti Yakubu maa o poga.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Zilipa dogi biduu ti Yakubu.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Liya yeli, “M mari zusuŋŋu.” Ka digili o yuuri Gadi.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Zilipa maali n dogi biduu ti Yakubu.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Liya yeli, “Wula sufupeelim ma bi mari? Pogiba ni boona ma sufupeelim.” Ka digili o yuuri Asa.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Chi cheebu jemani, Rubin nye dogim tiim vaari yi moo puu ti ti o ma Liya. Rachel yeli Liya, “Jaande, ti ma e biyaa dogim tiim vaari ŋa seesi.”

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Liya boosi u, “E n fa m sira ka che ma la, a na bi paali e nufu, e lee n ya n ti dee m bii dogim tiim vaari?” Rachel yeli u, “Ka e yi ti ma e biyaa dogim tiim vaari ŋa, e ni Yakubu ni tee n do yiiŋaa.”

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Zaanoori jemani, Yakubu n yi moo puu chena, Liya tuusi u soori, ka yeli u, “M nini n ya n do yiiŋaa, a kpali bo, m naani m bii dogim tiim vaari yo o lala samini.” O mari u do dindaari yiiŋu maa.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Naawun wumi Liya suusigu, o mari puwa, a dogi biduusi banu daana ti Yakubu.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Liya yeli, “Naawunni yo ma samini, a kpali ni naa m bipoga ti m sira zu.” Ka digili bii maa yuuri Isaka.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Ka yeli, “Naawunni pi ma ni piinisuŋŋu, pimparaŋa m sira ni tira ma jilima, a kpali biduusi bayoobu n na ma dogi n ti u.” Ka digili o yuuri Zebulun.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: A nyaaŋa, o dogi bipuŋŋu, ka digili o yuuri Dayina.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: o mari puwa, a dogi biduwa. Ka yeli, “Naawunni nyisi ma yi vi niŋi, o ti ma dogi biduwa.”

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: O digili o yuuri Yisifu, ka yeli, “Naawunni o na kpari n paasi ma biduwa.”

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rachel n dogi Yisifu nyaaŋa, Yakubu yeli Leban, “Basi ma soori m pori n gaa m yiri.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Naa m pogiba ni biisi seesi zu ni kpali m tumi i la ti ma, m gaari. E baŋŋi seema ni tumi i nene.”

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Yakubu yeli u, “E baŋŋi seema ni tumi n ti i, a ni seema e buŋkobiri n wasi m nuu niŋi.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Beela seesi e n daa n mari poin, ka ma ta, a paasiya ga. Seela zaa ni matira, Naawunni nafara i ni. Jemani paaya maa, m gbagba m nye m toori.”

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: O boosi u, “Bo n na e kpabira?” Yakubu yeli u, “Da ti ma sen. Ama, ka e ni sagi n niŋi yaso ni ya n yeli i ŋa, m na n kpari n nyaasira e buŋkobiri maa.

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Ti m kpe buŋkobiri maa puu gamisi a binchericherisi buusi ni peeri maa zaa, a ni pebiraasi, a loobi n toori. M sanyoori zaa n na la.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: O soosa zu, e ni nye maa m mari birikoŋsi koo m ka birikoŋsi. Jemanso e n ta a puwa, ka nye bimpeeli yi a niŋi, buu koo pee, a ni pe so n bi niŋi bimbiraa, ka wune, m zuuni n na la.”

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Leban yeli u, “M sagi seema e n yeli maa.”

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Dindaari maa zaa Leban gamisi budaasi ni bunyaana seesi n ba chericheri, a ni pebiraasi ni a binchericherisi zaa nyem ti o biisi, ba nyaasira a.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: O naa dabaa ata mari buŋkobiri maa chaŋŋi n gaa wogila, ka Yakubu nyaasira a seesi n che la.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Yakubu wa ti kpabi tibalisi ata, a ŋma a wulimaasa foli n foli a pegiri, boomabooma, a peligi n peligi.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Niŋi n na, o naa daari digili n digili buŋkobiri maa koom ŋuubu ziya, a kpali, buŋkobiri maa yi duurini taaba koom ŋuubu maa ziya.

ref: in front of the branches, and their young were spotted and speckled.
text: Buŋkobiri maa si n za daari maa see duura, a dogirini binchericherisi.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Yakubu barigi pesaasi maa yi Leban buŋkobiri maa niŋi. Ka penyaana maa ya n ti du, o yi tini a nyaasira Leban buŋkobseesi n ba chericheri maa. N ŋa n na Yakubu niŋi n barigi o buŋkobiri yi Leban toori niŋi.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Buŋkobnyaana seesi n mari kpeena si n ti duura, Yakubu yi naa ni daari maa digili a koom ŋuubu zii maa, a duura nisaaniŋi.

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Ama, a bimpugiri la wune si n ti duura, o bi diŋina daari maa niŋi. A zu, bimpugiri maa loobirini Leban toori, ka bi n kpeena la mi loobira Yakubu toori.

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: N ŋa sooraa n na Yakubu naa n loobi bindaana, a mari buŋkobdamta, a ni guruŋbipogiba, guruŋnachiinsi, nyogimasi, a ni buŋsi.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Dindaso n na Yakubu wumi maa Leban biisi togsirini n ŋa, “Yakubu naani ti ba bini zaa loobi o toori, a loobi daama daana.”

ref: Jacob also noticed that Laban was not as friendly as he had been before.
text: Ka Yakubu mi nye maa Leban yaniŋisa bi lee n ba seema o n daa n be la.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Niŋi n na, Duuma yeli Yakubu n ŋa. Pori n gaa e niriba saani, e yaadima tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, ka m ni ba e saani.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Yakubu ni wumi n ŋa, o tolimi n yeli Rachel ni Liya, ni ba ta o saani, seela o ni buŋkobiri n be la.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: O yeli ba, “M nyeya maa, m ni ya ba beebu bi lee n be seema o n daa n be la, ama m ba Naawunni la mi bani m saani.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Ya zaa mi nye seema ni peligi puu tumi ya ba.

ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: Ama, ya ba puni ma, o cherigi m sanyoori bupii zu, ama, Naawunni mi bi ti o nye daama niŋi ma daaŋu.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Jemani zaa ka Leban daa n si n ti yeli ni buŋkoochericherisi la n niŋi e sanyoobu, a zaa si n ya n dogi, a dogirini binchericherisi. O n lee n yeli ni pimparaŋa, buŋkobseesi niŋa n too booma n niŋi e sanyoori, buŋkobiri maa zaa yi lee n dogirini lala biisi.

ref: That's how God has taken sheep and goats from your father and given them to me.
text: A zu, Naawunni deeni ya ba buŋkobiri maa ti ma.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: “Buŋkobiri maa duubu jemani, m daa zaasini zaasim, a nye maa, gbeesi dima la daa n bani chericheri, ka seesi gba mari booma.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: Zaasim maa puwa, Naawunni malaaka booli yeli ma, ‘Yakubu.’ Ma sagi, ‘Mani n ŋa.’

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Malaaka maa yeli ma, ‘Nyaasi, gbeesi dima la n duura la bani chericheri, ka seesi niŋa mari booma, m nye bunso zaa Leban ni niŋini i.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Mani n niŋi Naawunni so n daa n nyisi m maŋŋa wusi i yi Betel la, seela e n daa n kpaa kpaam duligi kugiri, ka digili noori ti ma la. Pimparaŋa, yi kpe, a gaa tiŋgbanso niŋi ba n dogi i la.’”

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: M ba nyaasira ti ni maa saamba, o koosi ti di.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: A zu, bunso zaa Naawunni n dee n yi ti ba saani niŋini ti nini zaa biisi bini. A kpali lala zu, niŋi bunso zaa Naawunni n yeli e niŋi.”

ref: Then Jacob, his wives, and his children got on camels and left
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu naa o poga ni biisi ziili nyogimasi niŋi,

ref: northern Syria for the home of his father Isaac in Canaan. Jacob took along all his flocks, herds, and other property.
text: ka kpagi o buŋkobiri zaa pa yiya, a ni binseesi zaa o n daa nye, a yi Mesopoteemiya paaligu paasi n gaa o ba Yisaaka saani, Keenan paaligu.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Poin ka ba yi, Leban daa n wa ti ŋmaasirini o peeri koba, ka Rachel zu o ba laanta buga.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: A kpali lala zu, Yakubu daa n naani o bunso zaa zo n wa ti du mogikarigu, so ba n boona Yufireeti la, a naa o zu kpa Giliyadi pelli, seela zoori ni be ga la. Leban kari Yakubu bobiri

ref: Three days later Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
text: Yakubu n daa zo maa dabaa ta daari, poin ka Leban wumi maa o zoya.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Niŋi n na, Leban mari o niriba kari n pa Yakubu zu, dabaa ayapoin daari poin, ka o paag u, a yi Giliyadi paaligu, seela zoori n be ga maa.

ref: But God appeared to Laban in a dream that night and warned, “Don't say a word to Jacob. Don't make a threat or a promise.”
text: Dindaari yiiŋu, Naawunni nyisi o maŋŋa wusi Leban, a yi zaasim niŋi, ka yeli u, “Dasi, ka da pa togsi yatogidagu zaa ŋmeŋsi u.”

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Yakubu daa sabini o gbana suga yi Giliyadi zoori puwa, nisaaniŋi gbagba maa n na Leban gba ni o yidima sabi ba gbana suga.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Niŋi n na, Leban boosi Yakubu, “Wula zu n na e yomsi ma, a mari m bipunsi zo maa ba n gbaa daaba yi toowa niŋi.

ref: Why did you sneak away without telling me? I would have given you a going-away party with singing and with music on tambourines and harps.
text: Wula zu n na e sogi n zo, ka bi yeli ma? Ka e di kali ma ni, m di n naa binŋmeera ŋme, a yiimi n beeli i soori.

ref: You didn't even give me a chance to kiss my own grandchildren and daughters goodbye. That was really foolish.
text: E mi bi lee n ti ma mugisi m yaŋsi, ka kali ba ni ba madima, e wusi zolim ga.

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: M mari daama, m di n ti ba namsi i, ama, soosila yiiŋu, e ba Naawunni la, yeli ma ni m dasi, ka da pa ti togsi yatogibee zaa ti i.

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: M baŋiya maa e zoni, e ba yiri weebu yela n moog e nufu, ka wula n na e zu m yiri buga?”

ref: Jacob answered, “I left secretly because I was afraid you would take your daughters from me by force.
text: Yakubu yeli Leban n ŋa, “M daa zora i ni dabeem, ka fe maa, ka m yeli i, e ni fa e bipunsi.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Ka e nye e buga ŋa yi ti yindaana zaa saani, a daana bi daga o di nyevuri. Nyaasi m binaa zaa puwa, ka e yi nye bunso n niŋi e toori, e nyisima.” Yakubu mi daa n bi baŋŋi maa Rachel n zu o ba buga maa.

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Leban kpe Yakubu gbana suga dii, a yuligi n kpabira o buga, a ni Liya ni ba bipogiba diisi zaa n na o kpe. A nyaaŋa, o kpe Rachel sugiri puwa.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Rachel mi daa n naani o ba buga maa niŋi o nyogima kogu gbinniŋi, ka zi a niŋi. Niŋi n na, Leban nyaasi gbana sugiri maa zaa puu nyem, o bi nye bunso zaa.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Rachel yeli o ba Leban, “Jaande, da pa ti je suuri niŋi ma, a kpali ni bi nyisigi n za e yii zu, m pegirini.” Niŋi n na, Leban nyaasi seela zaa, ka bi nye o yiri buga maa.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Yakubu je suuri niŋi Leban, ka boosi u, “Yabee bo n na ma niŋi i, e kpali lala zu kari n pa m zu?

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Pimparaŋa, e n nyaasi m bini zaa puusi, bo n na e nye, ka o niŋi e toori? Bunso zaa e ni nye, ka o niŋi e toori, e naag u digili so zaa, a ni e niriba ba nye, ka nye so n mari yamonsi.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Ka moo puu buŋkoowa yi gbaag e buu koo pee, mani n yi yo o samini. Buŋkoowa so zaa ba n daa n yi zu, yiiŋu koo wuntooŋu, e yi tini ma yo o samini.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Jemanso wuluŋu ni waari n daa n kuura ma, ka ma daa n ki tee n gbisi.

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Ka Naawunni, so m ba dima, Ibrahim ni Yisaaka ni daa n tumina la, di ka m saani, e di kari ma basi nuyoosi, ama, Naawunni nye m nam, a ni seema ni tumi i, ka yiiŋu la, o kpaasi e tubiri.”

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Niŋi n na, Leban yeli Yakubu, “E pogiba ŋa niŋini m biisi, ka e biisi a gba mi niŋini m yaŋsi, buŋkobiri ŋa gba niŋini m bini, binseesi e nyaasira ŋa zaa niŋini m bini, ama pimparaŋa, wula ma lee n ya n niŋi m bipunsaa ni ba biisaa yela?

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: A zu, m niŋini siri ta, m nini ti digili noori ti taaba, o niŋi toŋine ti sunsuuniŋi.”

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu naa kugiri sabi, a duligi nisaaniŋi.

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: O n niŋi n ŋa baasi, o yeli o nyaaŋa dima, ni ba laŋsi kuga pa taaba niŋi. Ba laŋsi a pa taaba niŋi, ka ba zi n begi kuga maa, a di.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Leban daa n digilini nisaaniŋi yuuri Jaga Sahaduta, ka Yakubu gba mi digili nisaaniŋi yuuri Galiidi.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “This pile of rocks will remind us of our agreement.” That's why the place was named Galeed.
text: Niŋi n na, Leban yeli Yakubu, “Kuga ŋa n laŋsi n do ŋa n niŋi ti toŋine zinaa.” A zu n na ba daa n digili niŋi Galiidi la.

ref: Laban also said, “This pile of rocks means that the Lord will watch us both while we are apart from each other.” So the place was also named Mizpah.
text: Leban gba yeli, “Naawunni o nyaasira ti zaa jemanso ti n ya n barigi taaba ŋa.” A zu, o yuyindaana n daa n niŋi Mizipa.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Leban yeli, “Ka e si n namsira m biisaa, koo a naa poga paasi ba niŋi, halli ti n barigira taaba ŋa zaa, teesira maa Naawunni n nyaara ti.”

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Kuga ŋa n za n ti ti naŋgbandigiligu maa toŋinesi. M ki gali ŋama zaa nyaaŋa, a namsi i, e mi gba e da pa ta, a ti namsi ma.

ref: My father Nahor, your grandfather Abraham, and their ancestors all worshiped the same God, and he will make sure that we each keep the agreement. Then Jacob made a promise in the name of the fearsome God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: Ibrahim ni Naho Naawunni la ni di ti seriya.” Niŋi n na, Yakubu za Naawunni so, o ba Yisaaka ni daa tumina la, yuuri niŋi, a pe maa o ni pa ba naŋgbandigiligu maa niŋi.

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: O ku buŋkoowa nyisi sariga, a yi zoori maa zu, ka booli o dogiriba zaa ba ti di. Ba n ti di n baasi, ba do zoori maa puwa.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: A n kaa bee, Leban nyisigi, a mugisi o yaŋsi, a ni o bipunsi la, ka nafa ba, ka pori n kuli o yiri.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu daa lee n naa soori wara, Naawunni malaakasi ti paag u.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: O n si n nye ba, o yeli, “Naawunni ziiziya n ŋa.” Ka digili niŋi yuuri Mahanayim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Yatogso o n naa ti tumusi maa ba wa ti ti o beeri la n niŋi, ni ba yeli u, ni o nachiima, Yakubu, so n daa n ba Leban saani yuu, ka bi chena yiri la n na.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Ni o marini naagi, buŋsi, peeri, buusi, a ni gurunsi dabsi ni pogiba, ka o suusira i maa e zo u nimbaa.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Tumusi maa n pori n ta, n ŋa n na ba yeli Yakubu, “Ti wa ti yeli e beeri Isawu, o mari niriba kobsinaasi tuusira i.”

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Niŋi n na, dabeem ni sufusaaŋu gbaa Yakubu, a zu, o barigi o niriba la ni buusi, peeri, naagi, a ni nyogimasi tulum buyaayi zu.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: A kpali bo, o feni maa, ka Isawu ti zabi n nyaŋŋi tulum buluŋko, seesi n che la ni sogi n zo.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu suusi Naawunni, “M yaadaba Ibrahim ni m ba Yisaaka Naawunni, m yidaana, nyini n daa n yeli ma, ni m pori n gaa m paaligu, m niriba saani, e ni ti bunso zaa chaŋŋi nene ti ma.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: A zu, m suusira i ni e moligi ma yi m beeri Isawu nuusi niŋi. M zorini dabeem maa o chena o ti zabi m ni pogiba ni biisi toowa.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ama, nyini n daa n yeli ma, ni e n ti m loobi bindaana, ka ti m baligu saligi maa teeku puu tambisigu seesi di n ki tee n kaali.”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Yakubu daa doni niŋi kaa bee, ka nyisi binseesi o naa wa ti pi o beeri Isawu piini.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Ya daŋŋi n yeli u, “E nachiima Yakubu ba nyaaŋa chena.” Yakubu daa feni maa, “Ka o naa n ŋa piini pa yii wa ti ti o beeri Isawu, o puu ni peligi, ka o deeg u nuusi ayi.”

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli, “M nye Naawunni nufu niŋi, ka ma na mari nyevuri.” A zu, Yakubu digili niŋi yuuri Peniyel.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: O daa n nyisini buŋkobiri yiri zaa kpagira, ka yeli ba, “Ya pa ma yii wara, ka ti taaba lagima.”

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Niŋi n na, o yeli o guruŋtumusi maa yipaara, “Ka m beeri Isawu tuusi ya soori, ka boosi i, ‘E yidaana n niŋi ŋani?’ koo ‘Ya n na e wara?’ koo ‘Ŋani buŋkobiri n ŋa?’,

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: e yeli u, ‘A niŋini e nachiima Yakubu bini, o tumi ti, ni ti naa n ti ti i, ama o gba ba nyaaŋa chena.’”

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Lala yatogiraa gbagba n na o naa n ti tumusi bayi daana maa gba, a ni bata daana maa, ni ba zaa ba yeli o beeri Isawu.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: A zu, o ti ni piini ŋa pa yiya, ka ba do nisaaniŋi. Seema Yakubu ni Naawunni n daa n mo n ŋa

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: O n mari ba du mogiri maa baasi, o pori n ti mari o bunso zaa n che du.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: A nyaaŋa, o ko pori n ti ba o ziizii maa, dindaari yiiŋu maa. Dabso ti mari u moora, halli bee ti kaa.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Lala daba maa n nyaasi, ka o ki tee n nyaŋŋi Yakubu, o salli Yakubu kpiiligu, o kpiiligu maa furisi.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Niŋi n na, daba maa yeli Yakubu, “Cheli m gaari, bee kaaya.” Yakubu yeli u, “M ki ti e gaa, ama e nafa ma ni.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Daba maa boosi u. “E yuuri?” “M yuuri n niŋi Yakubu.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
