ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Yuma ayi nyaaŋa, Yijipiti paaligu naaba maa zaasi zaasim, ni o n za Naayel mogiri naŋgbanni.

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: Nagikara ayapoin n karigi n nyelisira yi mogiri maa puwa du n za, a ŋobira mogiri maa naŋgban moori.

ref: Then seven ugly, skinny cows came up out of the river and
text: A nyaaŋa, nagikogiri ayapoin n kogi n dagi gba yi mogiri maa puu du n ti za n begi ŋarima la.

ref: ate the fat, healthy cows. When this happened, the king woke up.
text: Nagiseesi n kogi la naa nagiseesi n karigi n nyelisira la vali. Niŋi n na, naaba maa yirimi n yoo.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: O lee n gbisira, ka zaasi, chi zu karasuma ayapoin n tomi gbinluŋko niŋi.

ref: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: A nyaaŋa, chi zu nyogirinyogirisi ayapoin, seesi wuluŋpusigu sauwa n pemsi n kuu gba yi.

ref: The thin heads of grain swallowed the seven full heads. Again the king woke up, and it had only been a dream.
text: Bin nyogirinyogirisi la naa bunsuma ayapoin la vali. Naaba maa lee n yirimi n yoo, ka zaasim n na.

ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
text: A boguuniŋi, sufusaaŋu gbaa u ga, o tumi n booli Yijipiti yambaŋiniba ni dibodibo niŋiniba. Naaba maa poli o zaasima maa wusi ba, ama, ba yindaana zaa bi baŋŋi a gbina yeli u.

ref: The king's personal servant said: Now I remember what I was supposed to do.
text: Niŋi n na, naaba maa buŋŋuura nyaasira kpeema la yeli u, “Zinni, m teesi seema ni bi niŋi n nene.

ref: When you were angry with me and your chief cook, you threw us both in jail in the house of the captain of the guard.
text: Dindaso, e n daa n je suuri niŋi m ni niso n niŋina e paanu, ka naa ti wa ti yo dii, a yi soojasi kpeema so n nyaasira i la laanta la.

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: Ti bayi maa zaa zaasi zaasima yiiŋluŋko, ka a mari gbina, a kuri.

ref: A young Hebrew, who was a servant of the captain of the guard, was there with us at the time. When we told him our dreams, he explained what each of them meant,
text: Hiburu bipolli yindaana daa n paasi ti niŋi, ka niŋi soojasi kpeema la guruŋu. Ti poli ti zaasima maa wusi u, o wusi ti so zaa o zaasim gbinni.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
text: Yela maa niŋi seema gbagba o n wusi ti a gbina maa, ba mari ma pori n ta m tuma niŋi, ka yagili m to maa daana o kpi.”

ref: The king sent for Joseph, who was quickly brought out of jail. He shaved, changed his clothes, and went to the king.
text: A zu, naaba maa tumi, ni ba wa ti mari Yisifu ta, noba maa niŋi. Ba wa ti nyisi Yisifu yi diyoorigu maa puwa. Yisifu puni o zu, ka cherigi o bimpinna, ka ta naaba maa saani.

ref: The king said to him, “I had a dream, yet no one can explain what it means. I am told that you can interpret dreams.”
text: Naaba maa yeli Yisifu, “M zaasini m zaasim, nira zaa mi bi baŋŋi o gbinni. Ama m wumiya maa, ka e n si n wumi zaasim, e ni baŋŋi o gbinni.”

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Yisifu yeli naaba maa, “M ki tee n wusi o gbinni, ama Naawunni n ni tee n wusi i o gbinni.”

ref: The king told Joseph: I dreamed I was standing on the bank of the Nile River.
text: Niŋi n na, naaba maa yeli Yisifu, “M zaasini, m ni za Naayel mogiri naŋgbanni,

ref: I saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river, and they began feeding on the grass.
text: ka nye nagikarisuma ayapoin n yi koom maa puu du n za n ŋobira moo.

ref: Next, seven skinny, bony cows came up out of the river. I have never seen such terrible looking cows anywhere in Egypt.
text: A nyaaŋa, nagikogiri dagiri ayapoin gba yi. M na bi nye lala nagikogiri taaba zaa yi Yijipiti paaligaa.

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Ama, a zaa nyaaŋa, nira zaa n ki baŋŋi maa a vali sen, a na si n kogini dagi seema a n kuri n be maa, ka ma yirimi n yoo.

ref: I also dreamed that I saw seven heads of grain growing on one stalk. The heads were full and ripe.
text: “M lee n zaasi n nye chi zuuri ayapoin n bi, a veela ga, ka tomi chi gbinluŋko niŋi.

ref: Then seven other heads of grain came up. They were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: A nyaaŋa, chi zu nyogirinyogiriseesi wuluŋpusigu sauwa n pemsi a kuu gba yi.

ref: These heads of grain swallowed the full ones. I told my dreams to the magicians, but none of them could tell me the meaning of the dreams.
text: Chi nyogirinyogirisi maa naa binsuma la vali. Ma poli a wusi dibodibo niŋiniba, ba yindaana zaa bi tee n yeli ma, a gbina.”

ref: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli naaba maa, “E zaasima maa zaa gbina niŋini luŋko, Naawunni nyisini wusi i bunso o n ya n niŋi.

ref: The seven good cows stand for seven years, and so do the seven good heads of grain.
text: “Nagisuma ayapoin la niŋini yuma ayapoin, ka chi zusuma ayapoin la mi niŋi yuma ayapoin, a gbina niŋini luŋko.

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Nagikogiri ayapoin, dagseesi n lee n yi la niŋini yuma ayapoin, lala n niŋi chi nyogirinyogirisi ayapoin seesi sauwa n yi sagbinni ti pemsi a kuu la gba. A niŋini kom yuma ayapoin.

ref: It is just as I said—God has shown what he intends to do.
text: “A si n niŋini maa seema ni yeli i la, naaba. Naawunni wusi i ni seema o n ya n niŋi.

ref: For seven years Egypt will have more than enough grain,
text: “Yuma ayapoin, bindira ni niŋi ga, a yi Yijipiti paaligaa.

ref: when the king made him governor, and he went everywhere for the king.
text: Yuma ayapoin nyaaŋa mini n lee n niŋi kom yuma, ya tami Yijipiti yuma ayapoin bindirigbaliŋu la yela. Kom maa ni saami Yijipiti paaligu zaa.

ref: The famine will be so bad that no one will remember that once there had been plenty.
text: Ba ni tami bindirigbaliŋu maa zaa yela, a kpali kom, so n ya n ta maa, ni niŋi aligaaya.

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: Bunso zu e n zaasi u buyi zu n niŋi, Naawunni niŋini siriya maa, a niŋi. A ki yuu, Naawunni ni ti lala niŋi.

ref: Your Majesty, you should find someone who is wise and will know what to do, so that you can put him in charge of all Egypt.
text: “Pimparaŋa, naaba, kpabi nivuso n mari yam, a baŋŋi yela, a naa Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu, a pobi o nuusi.

ref: Then appoint some other officials to collect one fifth of every crop harvested in Egypt during the seven years when there is plenty.
text: Kpabi tumtuminiba niŋi Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ba deegira yuma ayapoin bindira seesi n ya n niŋi ga la, bunu zu zaa luŋko.

ref: Give them the power to collect the grain during those good years and to store it in your cities.
text: Ba deegira bindira maa yumsuma seesi n chena ŋa, a zaali a nyini naaba kpeeŋu niŋi. Ba zaali a tiŋkarigu zaa puwa.

ref: It can be stored until it is needed during the seven years when there won't be enough grain in Egypt. This will keep the country from being destroyed because of the lack of food. Joseph Is Made Governor over Egypt
text: Bindira ŋa, ba deeg a, a zaali n guri paaligaa, ti ti naag a paasi ti niŋi, a yi kom yuma seesi n chena Yijipiti la niŋi. Lala jemani, kom maa ki muri paaligaa.” Ba naa Yisifu loobi Yijipiti paaligu zaa kpeema

ref: The king and his officials liked this plan.
text: Yaseesi Yisifu n daa yeli maa daa peri naaba maa ni o kpemba zaa.

ref: So the king said to them, “Who could possibly handle this better than Joseph? After all, the Spirit of God is with him.”
text: Naaba maa boosi ba, “Ti ni tee n nye nira maa dabaa, seema Naawunni sii n paali o niŋaa?”

ref: The king told Joseph, “God is the one who has shown you these things. No one else is as wise as you are or knows as much as you do.
text: Niŋi n na, naaba maa yeli Yisifu, “Seema Naawunni ni nyisi o yela zaa gbina wusi i ŋa, nira zaa ka niŋi, a baŋŋi Naawunni yela lala.

ref: I'm putting you in charge of my palace, and everybody will have to obey you. No one will be over you except me.
text: A zu, m ni naag i loobi kpeema, e kpebilim o pa m zu, e nyaasira m yiri bunso zaa, a ni m niriba. M niriba zaa ni wumi n tiri i, m naalim ko n ya n pa i yiya.”

ref: Then the king took off his royal ring and put it on Joseph's finger. He gave him fine clothes to wear and placed a gold chain around his neck.
text: Niŋi n na, naaba maa nyisi o naalim beŋŋu yi o nuwa, a niŋi Yisifu nuwa. Ka pili u bimpinsuma, ka naa suga jaagba niŋi o nuŋgori.

ref: He also let him ride in the chariot next to his own, and people shouted, “Make way for Joseph!” So Joseph was governor of Egypt.
text: Ka o ti ba naa Yisifu ziili binduurigusi turuku niŋi, a wusi maa so n pa o zu, ka ti niso n gutira u pa yiya, a tasi n yetira, ya ti soori, ya ti soori. Lala o daa n niŋi, a naa Yisifu loobi Yijipiti zaa kpeema.

ref: The king told Joseph, “Although I'm king, no one in Egypt is to do anything without your permission.”
text: Ka naaba maa daa n lee n yeli Yisifu, “Mani n niŋi Yijipiti paaligu zaa naaba, nira zaa ki tee n niŋi sen Yijipiti paaligaa zaa, ka e bi ti a daana soori.”

ref: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
text: Niŋi n na, naaba maa ti Yisifu Yijipiti yuuri, Zafenati-Paniiya, ka lee n ti u poga, Asenati, o ba n niŋi Potifera, so n niŋi Oon tindaana. Ka Yisifu daa n chaŋŋi n kaa Yijipiti paaligu zaa.

ref: For seven years there were big harvests of grain.
text: Yuma ayapoin, bindira daa n niŋi ga.

ref: Joseph collected and stored up the extra grain in the cities of Egypt near the fields where it was harvested.
text: Lala yuma maa, Yisifu daa laŋsini bindira maa zaali tiŋkara maa niŋi. Tiŋkarigu zaa puuri niŋi n na, o daa laŋsini bindira maa zaali.

ref: In fact, there was so much grain that they stopped keeping record, because it was like counting the grains of sand along the beach.
text: Bindira maa Yisifu n daa n laŋsira maa daa n ti niŋi zoogirisim, o daa n cheli a kaaligu, a daa n zooya ga, a maa teeku tambisigu.

ref: Joseph and his wife had two sons before the famine began.
text: Poin, ka kom maa daa n ti li, ka Yisifu ni o poga Asenati daa n dogini biduusi bayi.

ref: Their first son was named Manasseh, which means, “God has let me forget all my troubles and my family back home.”
text: Yisifu daa n digili o bikpeema maa yuuri Manaasi, ka yeli, “Naawunni ti ma tami nam la zaa, a ni m ba laanta niriba zaa yela.”

ref: His second son was named Ephraim, which means “God has made me a success in the land where I suffered.”
text: Yuso o n daa n lee n digili biisi bayi daana maa n niŋi, Efirem, ka yeli, “Naawunni ti ni ma nye dogim, a yi paaliguso ni nye nam.”

ref: Egypt's seven years of plenty came to an end,
text: Yuma ayapoin seesi bindira n daa n niŋi ga maa jemani n daa n ti gaari,

ref: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. There was not enough food in other countries, but all over Egypt there was plenty.
text: yuma ayapoin kom n daa n ti piili, a maa seema Yisifu ni daa n yeli la. Kom maa daa n ti li paaliguseesi paasi, ama Yijipiti paaligu wune, bindira daa n ba niŋi.

ref: When the famine finally struck Egypt, the people asked the king for food, but he said, “Go to Joseph and do what he tells you to do.”
text: A n daa n ti wumina Yijipiti dima, a yi kom pelli, ba daa n fobili n ti Yijipiti naaba, o yeli ba, “Ya we Yisifu saani, a wa ti niŋi bunso zaa o n yeli ya niŋi.”

ref: The famine became bad everywhere in Egypt, so Joseph opened the storehouses and sold the grain to the Egyptians.
text: Kom maa n daa n ti saligi seela zaa, Yisifu yoo o bindira diisi la, a nyisi chi maa koosi n tira Yijipiti dima.

ref: People from all over the world came to Egypt to buy grain, because the famine was so severe in their countries.
text: A kpali kom maa n daa n ba kpeeŋu ga seela zaa zu, niriba daa n yini paaliguseesi gba, daa n ti daara chi maa yi Yisifu saani.

ref: When Jacob found out there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why are you just sitting here, staring at one another?
text: Yakubu n daa n ti wumi ni bindira ba Yijipiti paaligu, o boosi o biduusi n ŋa, “Wula n na ya na zi n nyaasira taaba?”

ref: I have heard there is grain in Egypt. Go down and buy some, so we won't starve to death.”
text: O daa n lee n yeli ba, “M wumiya maa bindira ba Yijipiti, a zu, ya we niŋi, a wa ti da seesi ta, ti naa n guri ti zu, lala jemani, kom ki lee n ku ti.”

ref: Ten of Joseph's brothers went to Egypt to buy grain.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu beya pii we Yijipiti, ni ba wa ti da chi.

ref: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
text: Yakubu daa n bi ti ba sunzo Benjamin paa ba gaari, a kpali, o daa n zorini dabeem maa, o ni nye daaŋu.

ref: So Jacob's sons joined others from Canaan who were going to Egypt because of the terrible famine.
text: Kom maa daa n lini Keenan paaligu gba, a zu, Yakubu biduusi maa daa n paani niseesi we bindira maa daabu.

ref: Since Joseph was governor of Egypt and in charge of selling grain, his brothers came to him and bowed with their faces to the ground.
text: Yisifu mini n daa niŋi Yijipiti paaligu zaa kpeema, wune n daa n koosira chi maa tira paaligu maa dima. O beya maa n daa n ti paa Yijipiti, ba kpe Yisifu saani, a gba u tiŋŋa, ka zulisi ba zuuri.

ref: Joseph remembered what he had dreamed about them and said, “You're spies! You've come here to find out where our country is weak.”
text: Niŋi n na, o teesi o zaasima yi ba pelli, ka yeli ba, “Ya niŋini daatiriba, ya tani kpe, ya ti daari paaligaa, a nye seela ti pugirisim n be.”

ref: “No sir,” they replied. “We're your servants, and we have only come to buy grain.
text: Ba yeli u, “Akai, ti yidaana, pa lala n na, e gurunsi tani, ti ti da bindira.

ref: We're honest men, and we come from the same family—we're not spies.”
text: Ti zaa niŋini mabiluŋko, dabluŋko biisi, ti niŋini nivumansi, ti bi niŋi daatiriba.”

ref: “That isn't so!” Joseph insisted. “You've come here to find out where our country is weak.”
text: Yisifu yeli ba, “Akai, ya tani ya ti daari ti paaligaa, seela ti pugirisim n be.”

ref: But they explained, “Sir, we come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and one of our brothers is dead.”
text: Ba yeli u, “Tirima, e gurunsi, ti zaa daa n bani pii ni bayi, dabluŋko biisi, ka o zi Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, ti luŋko lee n ka niŋi, ka ti bibila daana mi ba ti ba saani.”

ref: Joseph replied: It's like I said. You're spies,
text: Yisifu yeli ba, “Wune n na ma si n yeli ya la, ya niŋini daatiriba.

ref: and I'm going to find out the truth. I swear by the life of the king that you won't leave this place until your youngest brother comes here.
text: N ŋa n na ma ya n niŋi ya yatoga ŋa nyaasi, a baŋŋi ya yamonsi. M pe Yijipiti naaba nyevuri maa, ya ki yi Yijipiti, ama ya sunzo la gba tani kpe.

ref: Choose one of you to go after your brother, while the rest of you stay here in jail. That will show whether you are telling the truth. But if you are lying, I swear by the life of the king that you are spies!
text: A zu, ya tumi ya yinni, o wa ti mari ya sunzo maa ta, ama, ba ni yo ya seesi n che la dii, lala jemani, ya yatoga puu n na, ti ni naa n baŋŋi maa yamonsi n na ya yetira. Ama, ka lala bi niŋi, a yi naaba maa nyevuri zu, ya niŋini daatiriba.”

ref: Joseph kept them all under guard for three days,
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu ti ba yo ba dii dabaa ata.

ref: before saying to them: Since I respect God, I'll give you a chance to save your lives.
text: Dabaa ata maa daari, Yisifu daa yeli ba, “M niŋini Naawunni zora, ya niŋi seema ni ya n wusiya ŋa, ka nye ya zu.

ref: If you are honest men, one of you must stay here in jail, and the rest of you can take the grain back to your starving families.
text: Ka ya niŋi yamonsi diriba, ya ti ya luŋko, o na ba diyoorigaa puwa, ka ya seesi n che la, ya naa chi pori n wa ti ti ya yidima, kom n kuura la.

ref: But you must bring your youngest brother to me. Then I'll know that you are telling the truth, and you won't be put to death. Joseph's brothers agreed
text: Ama, ya mari ya sunzobila la ti ti ma. N ŋa ni wusi maa yamonsi ba ya yatoga puwa. Lala jemani, ya ki kpi.” Ba sagi.

ref: and said to one another, “We're being punished because of Joseph. We saw the trouble he was in, but we refused to help him when he begged us. That's why these terrible things are happening.”
text: Ba yeli taaba, “O doni soori maa yaso ti n daa n niŋi ti sunzo Yisifu la zu n na, ti nye n ŋa tubidarigirisim la. Ti daa n nye nimbirimoso n daa n paag u, o suusi ti, ni ti cheli u, ka ti zagsi, lala nimbirimoo n paa ti gba la.”

ref: Reuben spoke up, “Didn't I tell you not to harm the boy? But you wouldn't listen, and now we have to pay the price for killing him.”
text: Ka Rubin boosi ba, “M daa n bi yeli ya, ni ya da pa ti niŋi biyaa sen, ka ya zagsi? To, bunso ti n daa n niŋi u la, o zeem maa samini n yoora ti la.”

ref: They did not know that Joseph could understand them, since he was speaking through an interpreter.
text: Ba daa bi baŋŋi maa Yisifu wumini ba yatoga la, a kpali, nira n daa n loobira ba yatoga maa tiri u.

ref: Joseph turned away from them and cried, but soon he turned back and spoke to them again. Then he had Simeon tied up and taken away while they watched. Joseph's Brothers Return to Canaan
text: Ba n daa n togsira, Yisifu gaa seela kumi, ka n lee n loobi n ti togsi ba saani. O daa n ti ni ba gbaa Simiyon yi ba puwa, a le u, ba zaa nufu niŋi. Yisifu beya pori n gaa Keenan

ref: Joseph gave orders for his brothers' grain sacks to be filled with grain and for their money to be put in their sacks. He also gave orders for them to be given food for their journey home. After this was done,
text: Yisifu ti ba niŋi chi niŋi o beya bowatesi niŋi, ka pori ba ligiri niŋi ba bowatesi puusi, ka lee n ti ba soori puu bindira. O n niŋi ba n ŋa baasi,

ref: they each loaded the grain on their donkeys and left.
text: ba kaasi ba ziiri pa ba buŋsi niŋi, a gbaa soori.

ref: When they stopped for the night, one of them opened his sack to get some grain for his donkey, and at once he saw his moneybag.
text: Ba n wa ti paa seela ba n ya n do n kaa bee, yindaana lerigi o bowate, ka o niŋi bindira ti o buŋŋu, ka nye o ligiri n pa saa.

ref: “Here's my money!” he told his brothers. “Right here in my sack.” They were trembling with fear as they stared at one another and asked themselves, “What has God done to us?”
text: O yeli o mabiisi la, “Ba pori m ligiri la niŋi ma, ŋarima n ba m bowate puu la.” Dabeem gbaa ba, ba niŋa ŋme taaba niŋi, ka ba boosi, “Bo yela n na Naawunni niŋi ti ŋa?”

ref: When they returned to the land of Canaan, they told their father Jacob everything that had happened to them:
text: Ba n ta ba ba Yakubu saani yi Keenan paaligu, ba yeli u yatogso zaa n paa ba, ba yeli,

ref: But we told him, “We're honest men, not spies.
text: Ama, ti yeli u maa, ti bi niŋi daatiriba, ti niŋini jilima dima.

ref: We come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and the other is dead.”
text: Ka yeli u, ti daa n bani pii ni bayi, dabluŋko biisi, yindaana lee n ka niŋi, ka ti bibila daana ba ti ba saani yi Keenan.

ref: Then the governor of Egypt told us, “I'll find out if you really are honest. Leave one of your brothers here with me, while you take the grain to your starving families.
text: Niŋi n na, dabso n niŋi Kpeema yi paaligu maa yeli ti, ‘N ŋa n na ma ni baŋŋi maa ya niŋini jilima niriba, ya ti ya yindaana o zi m saani, ka ya seesi n che la, ya naa chi la wara yiri, kom la zu.

ref: But bring your youngest brother to me, so I can be certain that you are honest men and not spies. After that, I'll let your other brother go free, and you can stay here and trade.”
text: Ama, ya mari ya sunzobila la ta, lala m ni baŋŋi maa, ya bi niŋi daatiriba, ama jilima niriba, ka m pori ya mabii ti ya, ka ya dira yawu yi kpe.’”

ref: When the brothers started emptying their sacks of grain, they found their moneybags in them. They were frightened, and so was their father Jacob,
text: Ba n daa n kpaagira ba chi yi ba bowatesi puwa, ba zaa nye ni ba ligiri borosi n ba niŋi. Jemanso ba ni ba ba n nye ligiri borosi maa, dabeem gbaa ba.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ba ba Yakubu yeli ba, “Ya naa m biisi, ka che ma. Yisifu lee n ka, Simiyon gba ka, pimparaŋa, ya lee n ya n naa Benjamin gaari. Mani n na bunso zaa ziina!”

ref: Reuben spoke up, “Father, if I don't bring Benjamin back, you can kill both of my sons. Trust me with him, and I'll bring him back.”
text: Rubin yeli o ba, “Ka m si n bi mari u pori n ta, e ku m biduusi bayi ŋa. Naag u pobi m nuusi, m ni mari u pori n ta.”

ref: But Jacob said, “I won't let my son Benjamin go down to Egypt with the rest of you. His brother is already dead, and he is the only son I have left. I am an old man, and if anything happens to him on the way, I'll die from sorrow, and all of you will be to blame.”
text: Ama Yakubu yeli u, “M biduwa ki pa ya zu gaa niŋi. O beeri kpiya, ka che o ko. Ka garidama ti gbaag u soori puwa, m zuaa peligini jaga, ya ni pi m ni sufusaaŋu.”

ref: The famine in Canaan got worse,
text: Kom maa daa n na ba kpeeŋu yi paaligu maa niŋi,

ref: until finally, Jacob's family had eaten all the grain they had bought in Egypt. So Jacob said to his sons, “Go back and buy some more grain.”
text: ba n daa n di ba chiseesi ba n daa n da n yi Yijipiti la baasi, ba ba yeli ba, “Ya lee n gaa n wa ti da bindira beela ti ti ti.”

ref: Judah replied, “The governor strictly warned us that we would not be allowed to see him unless we brought our youngest brother with us. If you let us take Benjamin along, we will go and buy grain. But we won't go without him!”
text: Ama, ka e yi ki sagi wune, ti ki gaari, a kpali, daba maa yeli ti, ni, ti ki lee n nye o nufusee, ka ti ni ti mabii maa bi na.”

ref: Jacob asked, “Why did you cause me so much trouble by telling the governor you had another brother?”
text: Yakubu boosi ba, “Bo n na ya yeli daba maa ni ya mabii na ba niŋi, a taasi ma garidama ŋa?”

ref: They answered, “He asked a lot of questions about us and our family. He wanted to know if you were still alive and if we had any more brothers. All we could do was answer his questions. How could we know he would tell us to bring along our brother?”
text: Ba yeli u, “Daba maa boosi ti ni boosa, maa o n baŋŋi ti ni ti laanta zaa yela. ‘Ya ba na ba niŋi? Ya na kpari n mari mabiya?’ Ti sagi. Wula n na ti ya n niŋi n baŋŋi, o ya n yeli, ya mari u paasi n ta?”

ref: Then Judah said to his father, “Let Benjamin go with me, and we will leave at once, so that none of us will starve to death.
text: Juuda yeli o ba Yakubu, “Naa bii maa pobi m nuusi, ti gaa pimparaŋa, ka ti nini, a ni ti biisi da kpi ni kom.

ref: I promise to bring him back safely, and if I don't, you can blame me as long as I live.
text: M gbagba ni za n ti o nyevuri moligirisim. Naag o tarijugu ziili ma. Ka m yi bi mari u ti ti i, ni alaanfiya, o tarijugu, o pa ma, m nyevuri puu zaa.

ref: If we had not wasted all this time, we could already have been there and back twice.”
text: Seema o n be ŋa, ka ti di bi nagimi, ti di gaaya, ka pori n ta buyi daana.”

ref: Their father said: If Benjamin must go with you, take the governor a gift of some of the best things from our own country, such as perfume, honey, spices, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text: Ba ba Yakubu yeli ba, “Ka lala wune n na, ya niŋi n ŋa, ya naa tiŋgbaŋaa binseesi wa ti pi u piini: kpasarigira beela, sikpaam beela, a ni binseesi n tira ziiri ŋusuma, tulalesi, a ni tiwalibiseesi n meesa.

ref: Also take along twice the amount of money for the grain, because there must have been some mistake when the money was put back in your sacks.
text: Ya naa ligiri tulumbuyaayi zu, ya wa ti yo seesi n daa n ba ya bowatesi puusi la. Saŋa, ba daa n naag a ni niŋi, ka bi baŋŋi.

ref: Take Benjamin with you and leave at once.
text: Ya mari ya mabii maa paasi n gaa yoom.

ref: When you go in to see the governor, I pray that God All-Powerful will be good to you and that the governor will let your other brother and Benjamin come back home with you. But if I must lose my children, I suppose I must.
text: Naawunni kpeeŋu zaa daana, o zo ya nimbaa yi daba maa saani. O ti ya mari ya mabii maa ni Benjamin zaa pori n ta. Ama, ma wune yi bi lee n nye ba gba, wune n na la.”

ref: The brothers took the gifts, twice the amount of money, and Benjamin. Then they hurried off to Egypt. When they stood in front of Joseph,
text: A zu, dabsi maa naa ba piinisi ni ligiri tulumbuyaayi, ka mari Benjamin gba paasi, ba niŋi yi n gaa Yijipiti, wa ti naa ba maŋŋa we Yisifu saani.

ref: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.”
text: Jemanso Yisifu n nye ba ni Benjamin la, o yeli o nachiima, “Mari niribaa gaari m laanta, ka korigi buŋkoowa, ba niŋi bindira, m ni ba n ya n di wuntooŋaa.”

ref: The servant did as he was told and took the brothers to Joseph's house.
text: Daba maa niŋi bunso zaa Yisifu ni yeli u la, o mari niriba maa gaa Yisifu laanta.

ref: But on the way they got worried and started thinking, “We are being taken there because of the money that was put back in our sacks last time. He will arrest us, make us his slaves, and take our donkeys.”
text: Dabeem daa n gbaani niriba maa, ba n mari ba gaari o laanta la. Ba feni maa, “Ba mari ti ni ta kpe kpali ti ligiri la n daa n ba ti bowatesi puusi la zu. O ya n zabini nyaŋŋi ti, ka naa ti loobi o gurunsi, a naa ti buŋsaa paasi.”

ref: So when they arrived at Joseph's house, they said to the servant in charge,
text: Ba wa ti togsi Yisifu nachiima maa saani, a yi laanta maa voori noori.

ref: “Sir, we came to Egypt once before to buy grain.
text: Ba yeli u, “Gaafira, ti n daa n ta kpe ti da bindira piiligu la,

ref: But when we stopped for the night, we each found in our grain sacks the exact amount we had paid. We have brought that money back,
text: soori puu seela n na, yiiŋu zibi ti, ti zaa lerigi ti bowatesi, ka nye ti ligiri n ba ti bowatesi naŋgbana niŋi, seema gbagba ti n da. A zu, ti mari a pori n ta.

ref: together with enough money to buy more grain. We don't know who put the money in our sacks.”
text: Ka ti na mari ligiri paasi n ta, ti ti daa bindira. Ti bi baŋŋi niso n naa ti ligiri pori n niŋi ti bowatesi niŋi.”

ref: “It's all right,” the servant replied. “Don't worry. The God you and your father worship must have put the money there, because I received your payment in full.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
text: O yeli ba, “A zaa veela, ya da zora dabeem, ya Naawunni, ya ba Naawunni n ti ya ligiri yi ya bowatesi niŋi, ma wune dee ya ligiri.” Niŋi n na, o mari Simiyon ti paasi ba niŋi.

ref: The servant took them into Joseph's house and gave them water to wash their feet. He also tended their donkeys.
text: Niŋi n na, nachiima la mari ba kpe Yisifu dii, ka ti ba koom ba pegi ba noba, ka lee n ti ba buŋsi bindira.

ref: The brothers got their gifts ready to give to Joseph at noon, since they had heard they were going to eat there.
text: Ba wumiya maa nisaaniŋi n na ba ya n di wuntooŋu maa. A zu, ba niŋi ba piinisi siriya gutira Yisifu, o ta.

ref: When Joseph came home, they gave him the gifts they had brought, and they bowed down to him.
text: Jemanso Yisifu n ta yiri, ba naa ba piiniseesi ba n ma n ta la ti u, ka gba u tiŋŋa, ka zulisi ba zuuri.

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
text: O boosi ba alaanfiya yela, ka boosi ba, “Ya ba, dakuriso yela ya n daa n yeli ma la, ba wula? O na ba o nyevuri niŋi?”

ref: They answered, “Your servant our father is still alive and well.” And again they bowed down to Joseph.
text: Ba yeli u, “E guruŋu, ti ba, na ba niŋi ni alaanfiya.” Ka ba gba u tiŋŋa jilimatiri.

ref: When Joseph looked around and saw his brother Benjamin, he said, “This must be your youngest brother, the one you told me about. God bless you, my son.”
text: O nyaasi ba poli n jili n ti nye o sunzo Benjamin, o ma bii gbagba, o boosi, “Ya sunzo so yela ya n yeli ma la n ŋa?” Ka yeli, “Naawunni nimbaa nuu o pa e niŋi, m biya.”

ref: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, “Serve the meal!”
text: A nyaaŋa, o pegi o nufu, ka yi laanta, a gbaag o maŋŋa, ka yeli, “Ya tori bindira la.”

ref: Joseph was served at a table by himself, and his brothers were served at another. The Egyptians sat at yet another table, because Egyptians felt it was disgusting to eat with Hebrews.
text: Ba ti u o toori, o ziizii kuri, ka ti o beya la gba, ba ziizii kuri, ka Yijipiti dimseesi o ni ba n dira la gba, ziizii kuri, a kpali Yijipiti dima ni Hiburu dima bi lagimi n dira, Yijipiti dima chisirini lala.

ref: To the surprise of Joseph's brothers, they were seated in front of him according to their ages, from the oldest to the youngest.
text: Ba daa n ti ni o beya maa zi n pa taaba zu, kpemba kpemba, o yiya, ba nyaasi taaba ni naŋgbaŋkuubu.

ref: They were served food from Joseph's table, and Benjamin was given five times as much as each of the others. So Joseph's brothers ate and drank with him and had a good time.
text: Ba ti ba bindira yi Yisifu teebul niŋi, Benjamin toori daa n gaa ba zaa toya tulum bunu zu. Ba nunu di, ka ŋu n kuuli.

ref: Later, Joseph told the servant in charge of his house, “Fill the men's grain sacks with as much as they can hold and put their money in the sacks. Also put my silver cup in the sack of the youngest brother.” The servant did as he was told.
text: Ka naa m gbiti kobee la, a ni ligiseesi ba sunzobila la n di n ya n naa da bindira maa paasi n niŋi o bindira bowate maa puwa.” Niŋi n na, nachiima maa niŋi seema Yisifu n yeli u maa.

ref: Early the next morning, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
text: Bee n daa n ti chee, bogufiili, o beya maa niŋi ba buŋsi siriya, a gbaa yiri soori.

ref: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, “Go after those men! When you catch them, say, ‘My master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
text: Ba n daa n yi tiŋkarigu maa wara, ba daa n na bi paa wogila, ka Yisifu yeli o nachiinsi kpeema maa n ŋa, “Zo yomyom kari niriba la bobiri. Ka e paa ba, e boosi ba, ‘Wula n na ya naa yabeeri yoora yasuma samini?

ref: Not only does he drink from his cup, but he also uses it to learn about the future. You have done a terrible thing.’ ”
text: Wula n na ya zu m yidaana gbiti kobee? Wune n na o ma n ŋuura bini, ka lee n naag u baŋina soosizu yela. Ya niŋi yabee ga.’”

ref: When the servant caught up with them, he said exactly what Joseph had told him to say.
text: Nachiinsi kpeema maa n wa ti paa ba, o yeli ba seema gbagba o yidaana n yeli u la.

ref: But they replied, “Sir, why do you say such things? We would never do anything like that!
text: Niŋi n na, ba yeli u, “Bo n na e yeli ti lala? Ti niŋini e gurunsi, ti ki sagi n niŋi lala!

ref: We even returned the money we found in our grain sacks when we got back to Canaan. So why would we want to steal any silver or gold from your master's house?
text: Ti n daa we ti paa ti yiri, ka nye ti ligiri, ti daa n bi lee n pori a yi Keenan zaa ti ti ya? Ka wula zu n na ti ya n zu e yidaana gbiti koo suga, a yi o yiri?

ref: If you find that one of us has the cup, then kill him, and the rest of us will become your slaves.”
text: Ka e yi nye u yi ti yindaana zaa saani, kum n na a daana ni kpi, ka ti seesi n che la, ti mi loobi m yidaana daaba.”

ref: “Good!” the man replied, “I'll do what you have said. But only the one who has the cup will become my slave. The rest of you can go free.”
text: O yeli ba, “M wumiya. Ama ya so saani ti n nye kobeegaa ko ni loobi m daabiri, ka ya seesi n che la, ya nye ya zu.”

ref: Each of the brothers quickly put his sack on the ground and opened it.
text: A zu, ba zaa lerigi ba bowatesi yi ba buŋsi niŋi yoom zaali tiŋŋa, ka lerigi a noya.

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: Yisifu nachiinsi kpeema maa yuligira ba ziiri puusi nyaasira, ba kpeema daana ziiri niŋi n na o piili yuligi, o yuligi lala a ti paa bibila daana, Benjamin. O bowate puu n na o nye gbiti kobee maa.

ref: This upset the brothers so much that they began tearing their clothes in sorrow. Then they loaded their donkeys and returned to the city.
text: O beya maa darigi ba bimpinna kasigi, a kpali sufusaaŋu zu, ka ba lee n le ba ziirisi maa pa ba buŋsi niŋi, a pori n gaa tiŋŋa maa puwa.

ref: When Judah and his brothers got there, Joseph was still at home. So they bowed down to Joseph,
text: Juuda ni o sunzoba maa n daa n pori n we tiŋŋa maa puwa, ba we Yisifu yiri, a wa ti do u tiŋŋa.

ref: “Sir, what can we say?” Judah replied. “How can we say we are innocent, when God has shown we are guilty? And now all of us are your slaves, especially the one who had the cup.”
text: Niŋi n na, Juuda yeli, “Pimparaŋa, bo n na ti lee n mari ti yeli i, ti yidaana? Wula ti lee n ya n yeli, a wusi maa ti bi naa kobee maa? Naawunni yuligi ti gbinni, ti yabee yi paaligu, ti zaa ni loobi e daaba, pa so saani e ni nye gbiti kobee maa ko.”

ref: Joseph told them, “I would never punish all of you. Only the one who was caught with the cup will become my slave. The rest of you are free to go home to your father.” Judah Pleads for Benjamin
text: Yisifu yeli ba, “Akai! M ki niŋi lala. So saani ba n nye kobee la yi o ziiri niŋi maa ko n ni loobi m daabiri. Yaseesi n che la, ya pori n kunna ya ba saani, a ni niŋgbirimaasim.” Juuda suusi Yisifu Benjamin

ref: Judah went over to Joseph and said: Sir, you have as much power as the king himself, and I am only your slave. Please don't get angry if I speak.
text: Niŋi n na, Juuda we o saani, a wa ti yeli u n ŋa, “M yidaana, jaande, cheli e daabiri togsi yaluŋko ti i. Da ti e suuri nyisigi, m baŋŋi e ni Yijipiti naaba zaa n sagi.

ref: You asked us if our father was still alive and if we had any more brothers.
text: Nyini n daa n boosi ti, ni ti mari ba ni sunzo?

ref: So we told you, “Our father is a very old man. In fact, he was already old when Benjamin was born. Benjamin's brother is dead. Now Benjamin is the only one of the two brothers who is still alive, and our father loves him very much.”
text: Ti sagi, Mm, ti mariya, ti ba kpe kpebilim niŋi, ka dogi biduwa o kurigirisim jemani. Bii maa beeri kpiya, o ko n che o ma biya, ka o ba kpabira u ga.

ref: You ordered us to bring him here, so you could see him for yourself.
text: Niŋi n na, e daa n yeli ti, ni ti mari u ta, e nye u.

ref: But you warned us that we could never see you again, unless our youngest brother came with us.
text: Ama, e daa n yeli ti, ni, ka ti bi mari ti sunzo maa ta e saani, ti nini nimbiisi ki lee n niŋi anaasi.

ref: So we returned to our father and reported what you had said.
text: Ti n daa n pori n gaari ti ba, e daabiri, saani, ti yeli u seema zaa e n yeli.

ref: Later our father sent us back here to buy more grain.
text: Niŋi n na, ti ba yeli ti, ya pori n wa ti da bindira ta.

ref: But we told him, “We can't go back to Egypt without our youngest brother. We will never be let in to see the governor, unless he is with us.”
text: Ti yeli u, ‘Ti ki tee n gaa. Ka e bi ti ti sunzobilaa pa ti zu, ti ki nye daba maa nufu see.

ref: Sir, our father then reminded us that his favorite wife had given birth to two sons.
text: Ti ba yeli ti, ya baŋiya maa, m poga Rachel dogini biduusi bayi ko.

ref: One of them was already missing and had not been seen for a long time. My father thinks the boy was torn to pieces by some wild animal,
text: Luŋko lee n ka m saani, a ko moo puu buŋ n gbaag u. O n yi zaa, m bi lee n nye u.

ref: and he said, “I am an old man. If you take Benjamin from me, and something happens to him, I will die of a broken heart.”
text: Ka ya lee n nyisi ŋa la mi yi m saani, ka garidama wa ti paa soori, sufusaaŋu ni ku ma, m kurigirisim niŋi.

ref: I promised my father that I would bring him safely home. If I don't, I told my father he could blame me the rest of my life.
text: “M yidaana, poin ka ti ta kpe, mani, e daabiri, daa n naani m nyevuri pa tarima, biyaa zu, a yi m ba saani, maa ka biyaa yi bi lee n pori n ta, m ni zi o tarijugu, m beebu niŋi zaa.

ref: Sir, I am your slave. Please let me stay here in place of Benjamin and let him return home with his brothers.
text: A zu, jaande, cheli m dee bii maa noba, a ba kpe, a loobi e daabiri, ka bii maa o paa o beya ŋa pori n kunna.

ref: How can I face my father if Benjamin isn't with me? I couldn't bear to see my father in such sorrow.
text: A kpali bo? Wula ma ya n pori n gaa m ba saani, ka che biyaa? M ki n ti n ŋa sufusaaŋu kpe m ba niŋi.”

ref: Since Joseph could no longer control his feelings in front of his servants, he sent them out of the room. When he was alone with his brothers, he told them, “I am Joseph.”
text: A jemani maa, Yisifu bi lee n tee n mari o maŋŋa yi o nachiinsi maa saani, ka chaari kpeeŋutiri yeli ba, “So zaag o yi m saani.” A zu, nira zaa daa n lee n ka Yisifu saani, jemanso o naag o maŋŋa wusi o mabiisi maa.

ref: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
text: Ka chaari kpeeŋutiri, haali Yijipiti dima wumi, ka Yijipiti naaba laanta dima wumi o baaru.

ref: Joseph told them to come closer to him, and when they did, he said: Yes, I am your brother Joseph, the one you sold into Egypt.
text: Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa, “Ya vuu n ta m saani.” Ba n niŋi lala, o yeli ba, mani n niŋi ya sunzo Yisifu, so ya n koosi n ti Yijipiti dima la.

ref: Don't worry or blame yourselves for what you did. God is the one who sent me ahead of you to save lives.
text: Ama pimparaŋa, ya da ti ya sufusi saami, koo, a yeli maa ya bi niŋi nene, ya n koosi ma kpe ŋa. A kpali, ya nyevuya moligirisim zu n na, Naawunni ti ma pa ya yii ta kpe.

ref: There has already been a famine for two years, and for five more years no one will plow fields or harvest grain.
text: Yuma ayi n na la, kom n ba tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, ka yuma anu seesi n che, koobu ki ba niŋi, cheebu gba ki ba niŋi.

ref: But God sent me on ahead of you to keep your families alive and to save you in this wonderful way.
text: Naawunni mari ma ni pa ya yii ta, m ti nye bunso zaali ya tiŋgbaŋaa niŋi, a moligi ya ni ya nyaaŋa dima, ya nye moligirisim tintaa.

ref: After all, you weren't really the ones who sent me here—it was God. He made me the highest official in the king's court and placed me over all Egypt.
text: “Ama, pa yarima n mari ma ta kpe, wune Naawunni n daa n naa ma loobi Yijipiti naaba feretira, a ni o laanta zaa kpeema, ka nyaasira Yijipiti paaligu zaa.

ref: Now hurry back and tell my father that his son Joseph says, “God has made me ruler of Egypt. Come here as quickly as you can.
text: Pimparaŋa, ya pori yomyom gaari m ba saani, wa ti yeli u, ‘N ŋa n na, e bii Yisifu yeli. Naawunni naa ma ni loobi yidaana, a yi Yijipiti zaa. Ta m saani, ka da nagimi.

ref: You will live near me in the region of Goshen with your children and grandchildren, as well as with your sheep, goats, cattle, and everything else you own.
text: Ya ti zi Goosin paaligu, a leeli ma, ya ni ya biisi, ya yaŋsi, peeri, naagi, a ni bunso zaa ya n mari.

ref: I will take care of you there during the next five years of famine. But if you don't come, you and your family and your animals will starve to death.”
text: M ni tira ya bindira nisaaniŋi, a kpali, yuma anu gba kom na ba niŋi. Ka pa lala, ya ni ya laanta zaa ni ya bunso zaa ni kpini kom.’”

ref: All of you, including my brother Benjamin, can tell by what I have said that I really am Joseph.
text: Yisifu lee n yeli, “Ya gbagba nyeya, lala n niŋi m sunzo Benjamin gba, a maa o niŋini yamonsi, ni mani so n togsira ya saani n niŋi Yisifu.

ref: Tell my father about my great power here in Egypt and about everything you have seen. Hurry and bring him here.
text: Ya yeli m ba, m yela, jilimaso ni mari yi Yijipiti paaligu, a ni yaso zaa ya n nye. Ka ya mari m ba ta kpe yomyom.”

ref: Joseph was still crying as he kissed each of his other brothers. After this, they started talking with Joseph.
text: Ka mugisira o mabiisi maa, ka kumina. A nyaaŋa, o ni ba togsi taaba saani.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Niŋi n na, baaru maa paa naaba maa yiri, maa Yisifu mabiisi tani. A daa n peri naaba maa ni o kpemba zaa.

ref: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
text: O yeli Yisifu, “Yeli e mabiisi maa, ‘Ya niŋi n ŋa, ya naa ya bindira le n pa ya buŋsi niŋi, a pori n gaa Keenan paaligu,

ref: Tell them to bring their father and their families here. I will give them the best land in Egypt, and they can eat and enjoy everything that grows there.
text: a wa ti mari ya ba ni ya pogiba ni ya biisi pori n ta m saani. M ni ti ya tiŋgbanmaŋa yi Yijipiti, ya di o tono ni meesim.’

ref: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
text: Ka e lee n yeli ba, ‘Ya niŋi n ŋa, ya naa turukusi yi Yijipiti, wa ti naa ya biisi, pogiba, a ni ya ba ta.

ref: They can leave their possessions behind, because they will be given the best of everything in Egypt.
text: Ya da ti ya bini yela tiri ya, tiŋgbaŋaa binsuma zaa ni niŋi ya toori.’”

ref: Jacob's sons agreed to do what the king had said. And Joseph gave them wagons and food for their trip home, just as the king had ordered.
text: Lala jemani maa, Yakubu biisi niŋi seema ba n yeli ba maa. Yisifu gba naa turukusi ti ba, a maa seema naaba maa n yeli la, ka lee n ti ba soori puu bindira.

ref: Joseph gave some new clothes to each of his brothers, but to Benjamin he gave five new outfits and
text: O ti ba so zaa bimpinna luŋkoluŋko, ama Benjamin wune, o ti u gbitisi kobsita, a ni bimpinna tulumbunu zu.

ref: To his father he sent ten donkeys loaded with the best things in Egypt, and ten other donkeys loaded with grain and bread and other food for the return trip.
text: Binseesi o n naa ti ba wa ti ti o ba n ŋa, buŋsi pii zi Yijipiti bimmansi, ka naa chi ni paanusi ziili buŋnyaana piiya, a ni o ba soori puu bindira.

ref: Then he sent his brothers off and told them, “Don't argue on the way home!”
text: Niŋi n na, o beeli o mabiisi maa soori. Ba n wara maa, o yeli ba, “Ya wara, ka da zabira.”

ref: Joseph's brothers left Egypt, and when they arrived in Canaan,
text: Lala jemani, ba yi Yijipiti ta ba ba Yakubu saani yi Keenan paaligu.

ref: they told their father that Joseph was still alive and was the ruler of Egypt. But their father was so surprised that he could not believe them.
text: Ba yeli u, “Yisifu na ba niŋi! Wune n niŋi Yijipiti paaligu zaa nyaasira.” Yakubu fere jebi u, o bi yeride ba.

ref: Then they told him everything Joseph had said. When he saw the wagons Joseph had sent, he felt much better
text: Ama, jemanso ba n yeli u bunso zaa Yisifu n yeli ba, ka o lee n nye Yisifu n naa turukusi ti ba ti mari u pori n gaari, ba ba Yakubu sii pori n ta.

ref: and said, “Now I can believe you! My son Joseph must really be alive, and I will get to see him before I die.”
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu yeli, “M wumi o gbinni, m bii Yisifu na ba niŋi. M ni wa ti nye u, poin ka kpi.”

ref: Jacob packed up everything he owned and left for Egypt. On the way he stopped near the town of Beersheba and offered sacrifices to the God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: A zu, Yakubu nyisigi n naa o bunso zaa o n mari, a wara. O n wa ti paa Beesiiba, o nyisi sariga ti Naawunni, so o ba Yisaaka n daa n tumina la.

ref: That night, God spoke to him in a dream and said, “Jacob! Jacob!” “Here I am,” Jacob answered.
text: O yiiŋu, Naawunni togsi Yakubu saani yi zaasim niŋi, a booli, “Yakubu, Yakubu.” O sagi, “Mani n ŋa.”

ref: God said, “I am God, the same God your father worshiped. Don't be afraid to go to Egypt. I will give you so many descendants that one day they will become a nation.
text: O yeli u, “Mani n niŋi Naawunni, so e ba n daa n tumina. Da zora dabeem, a yi Yijipiti weebu. M ni loobi i balitintaa nisaaniŋi.

ref: I will go with you to Egypt, and later I will bring your descendants back here. Your son Joseph will be at your side when you die.”
text: M ni paag i gaa Yijipiti, ka lee n mari e yaŋsi pori n ta paaligaa. Yisifu nuusi niŋi n na e ni kpi.”

ref: The king will call you in and ask what you do for a living.
text: Ka naaba maa booli ya boosi, ‘Bo tuma n na ya tumina?’

ref: Sixty-six members of Jacob's family went to Egypt with him, not counting his daughters-in-law.
text: Niseesi n daa pa Yakubu zu gaa Yijipiti, seesi n niŋi o biisi ni yaŋsi, ba pisiyoobu ni bayoobu. O biisi pogiba bi paasi.

ref: Jacob had sent his son Judah ahead of him to ask Joseph to meet them in Goshen.
text: Yakubu tumi Juuda, o pa u yii gaa Yisifu saani, a wa ti yeli u, o ti wusi ma Goosin soori. Ba n paa Goosin paaligu,

ref: So Joseph got in his chariot and went to meet his father. When they met, Joseph hugged his father around the neck and cried for a long time.
text: Yisifu niŋi o turuku seriya, a gaa Goosin wa ti paag o ba Yakubu. O n wa ti paag u, o naag o nuusi wa ti che n vili o ba nuŋgori, ka kumi jemanwogili.

ref: Jacob said to Joseph, “Now that I have seen you and know you are still alive, I am ready to die.”
text: Yakubu yeli Yisifu, “Pimparaŋa m niŋi siriya m kpi, seema m gbagba n si n nye i, ka baŋŋi maa e na ba nyevuri puwa.”

ref: Then Joseph said to his brothers and to everyone who had come with them: I must go and tell the king that you have arrived from Canaan.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa ni o ba laanta zaa, “M ni gaa naaba maa saani wa ti yeli u, ‘M mabiisi ni m ba ni o laanta zaa, seesi n daa n ba Keenan paaligu la ta m saani.

ref: I will tell him that you are shepherds and that you have brought your sheep, goats, cattle, and everything else you own.
text: Dabsi maa niŋini pekpagiriba, ba guunini buŋkobiri. Ba marini ba peeri ni buŋkobiseesi ni bunso zaa ba n mari ta.’

ref: When he does, be sure to say, “We are shepherds. Our families have always raised sheep.” If you tell him this, he will let you settle in the region of Goshen. Joseph wanted them to say this to the king, because the Egyptians did not like to be around anyone who raised sheep.
text: Ya yeli u, ‘E gurunsi guunini buŋkobiri ti bibiilim niŋi zaa, seema ti ba dima n niŋina.’ Lala wune, o ni sagi ya zi Goosin paaligu, a kpali peeri kpagirisim niŋini tumdagiri ti Yijipiti dima zu.”

ref: who asked them, “What do you do for a living?” “Sir, we are shepherds,” was their answer. “Our families have always raised sheep.
text: Naaba maa boosi ba, “Bo tuma n na ya tumina?” Ba yeli u, “E gurunsi niŋini pekpagiriba, a maa ti ba dima.”

ref: But in our country all the pastures are dried up, and our sheep have no grass to eat. So we, your servants, have come here. Please let us live in the region of Goshen.”
text: Ka ba lee n yeli u, “Ti ya n ti zinni paa jemani yinni, a kpali kom maa ba kpeeŋu ga yi Keenan, ka e gurunsi peeri ka bindira. A zu, jaande ti e gurunsi ba zi Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.”

ref: The king said to Joseph, “It's good that your father and brothers have arrived.
text: Naaba maa yeli Yisifu, “E ba ni e mabiisi ta e saani,

ref: I will let them live anywhere they choose in the land of Egypt, but I suggest that they settle in Goshen, the best part of our land. I would also like for your finest shepherds to watch after my own sheep and goats.”
text: Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu zaa bani e nuusi niŋi, ti e ba ni e mabiisi maa ba zi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zimaŋŋa ziya. Ti ba zi Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi. Ka e baŋŋi ba seesi n baŋŋi buŋkobiri yela nene, e ti ba nyaasira m gba buŋkobiri ŋa.”

ref: Then Joseph brought his father Jacob and introduced him to the king. Jacob gave the king his blessing,
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu mari o ba Yakubu ti wusi naaba maa. Ka Yakubu dibi n ti naaba maa.

ref: and the king asked him, “How old are you?”
text: Naaba maa boosi u, “E yuma a la?”

ref: Then Jacob gave the king his blessing once again and left.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu lee n maali n dibi n ti naaba maa, ka yi o saani.

ref: Joseph obeyed the king's orders and gave his father and brothers some of the best land in Egypt near the city of Rameses.
text: A zu, Yisifu ziili o ba ni o mabiisi yi Yijipiti, a ti ba bindaalim yi tiŋgbaŋŋu maa zimaŋŋa ziya, Ramasi paaligu, seema naaba maa n wusi.

ref: So Joseph sold them the grain that had been stored up, and he put the money in the king's treasury.
text: Yisifu n koosira bindira maa, o dee Yijipiti ni Keenan dima ligiri zaa, a naag a wa ti ti naaba maa.

ref: But when everyone had run out of money, the Egyptians came to Joseph and demanded, “Give us more grain! If you don't, we'll soon be dead, because our money's all gone.”
text: Jemanso Yijipiti ni Keenan dima ligiri n baasi, Yijipiti dima zaa ta Yisifu saani ti yeli u, “Ti ti bindira, a daga ti n kpi e nufu a niŋi? Ti ligiri zaa baasiya.”

ref: “If you don't have any money,” Joseph answered, “give me your animals, and I'll let you have some grain.”
text: Yisifu yeli ba, “Ya ligiri n baasi a, ya mari ya buŋkobiri ti tagira.”

ref: Then the people came to him and said: Sir, there's no way we can hide the truth from you. We are broke, and we don't have any more animals. We have nothing left except ourselves and our land.
text: Lala yumini maa n ti gaari, yumiso n lee n pa, ba ta o saani ti yeli u, “Ti ki tee n naa yamonsi sogi ti yidaana niŋi, ti ligiri zaa baasiya, ka ti buŋkobiri zaa loobi e toori, ti bi lee n mari, ti ti tiyidaana, ama ti maŋsi, a ni ti tiŋgbana.

ref: Don't let us starve and our land be ruined. If you'll give us grain to eat and seed to plant, we'll sell ourselves and our land to the king. We'll become his slaves.
text: Wula zu n na ti ya n kpi e nufu niŋi, ti ni ti tiŋgbana zaa, Ti ni naa ti maŋsi ni ti tiŋgbana tagi bindira, ka ti ni ti tiŋgbana zaa, a loobi naaba maa toori. Ti ti bindira, ka ti da kpi, ka che tiŋgbaŋŋu yoo digili.”

ref: except the priests. The king gave the priests a regular food allowance, so they did not have to sell their land.
text: Ama, o daa n bi da tiŋgbana maaniba la tiŋgbana, a kpali naaba maa daa n yi yoora ba ni ligiri, a puu n na ba daa n nyaara ba bindira. Bunso n ti ba bi koosi ba tiŋgbaŋŋu n na la.

ref: Then Joseph said to the people, “You and your land now belong to the king. I'm giving you seed to plant,
text: Yisifu yeli niriba maa, “Pimparaŋa ni da ya ni ya tiŋgbana ti naaba maa ŋa. Bumbura n na la, ya naa n wa ti buri.

ref: but one fifth of your crops must go to the king. You can keep the rest as seed or as food for your families.”
text: Ama, ka a ti niŋi, ya che, ya digili a bunu zu, naaba maa n su buluŋko zu. Bunaasi seesi n che la, ya naag a zaali ya ni ya pogiba ni biisi bindira ni bumbura.”

ref: “Sir, you have saved our lives!” they answered. “We are glad to be slaves of the king.”
text: Ba yeli u, “Ti yidaana, e moligi ti nyevuya. Ka a peri i, ti ni loobi naaba maa daaba.”

ref: Then Joseph made a law that one fifth of the harvest would always belong to the king. Only the priests did not lose their land. Jacob Becomes an Old Man
text: A zu, Yisifu piili u maa zaaligu, a wara Yijipiti tiŋgbaŋŋu pelli, a ti paa zinni, o na tuminini, a maa bindira bunu daana niŋini naaba maa toori. Tiŋgbana maaniba la ko tiŋgbaŋŋu n daa bi niŋi naaba maa toori. Bunso Yakubu n suusi

ref: The people of Israel made their home in the land of Goshen, where they became prosperous and had large families.
text: A jemani, Izireel dima daa n zinni Yijipiti paaligu, Goosin tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi. Ba nye kapete bini niŋi, ka dogi bidamta.

ref: When Jacob knew he did not have long to live, he called in Joseph and said, “If you really love me, you must make a solemn promise not to bury me in Egypt.
text: Yakubu kum jemani n daa n ti paagira, o booli o bii Yisifu ti yeli u, “Ka e ni sagi n ti ma, e naag e nuu tabi m kpiiligu tiŋini, ka digili noori maa e ni wusi ma kpabirisim ni yamonsitiri. Da pa ti pi ma yi Yijipiti.

ref: Instead, bury me in the place where my ancestors are buried.” “I will do what you have asked,” Joseph answered.
text: Ama ka m pa ti kpi, ya zi ma yi, a wa ti pi ma yi m ba dima noba see.” O yeli u, “M ni niŋi i seema e n yeli maa.”

ref: “Will you give me your word?” Jacob asked. “Yes, I will,” Joseph promised. After this, Jacob bowed down and prayed at the head of his bed.
text: Yakubu yeli u, “Pe n ti ma.” Niŋi n na, Yisifu pe n ti u, ka Yakubu zulisi o zu o bindoori niŋi.

ref: Both the cave and the land that goes with it were bought from the Hittites.
text: Tiŋgbaŋŋu maa ni zovoori maa zaa ba daa n da n yi Hititi niriba saani.”

ref: When Jacob had finished giving these instructions to his sons, he lay down on his bed and died.
text: Yakubu n togsi n ŋa ti o biisi baasi, o laasi o noba du bindoori niŋi, ka vuusi o baasigu vuusim, a gaari o yaadima saani.

ref: Joseph started crying, then leaned over to hug and kiss his father.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu li n pa o ba niŋi, a kumi, ka mugisi u.

ref: When the time of mourning was over, Joseph said to the Egyptian leaders, “If you consider me your friend, please speak to the king for me.
text: Jemanso kuuri kuminisim n ti baasi, Yisifu yeli naaba maa kpemba, “Ka ya n zo ma nimbaa, ya togsi naaba maa saani ti ma, ya yeli u,

ref: Just before my father died, he made me promise to bury him in his burial cave in Canaan. If the king will give me permission to go, I will come back here.”
text: ‘Poin ka m ba kpi, o ti ma digili noori maa, m pi u yi seela o n gbi o kaa, a yi Keenan paaligu. Jaande, ka m wa ti pi m ba, ka pori n chena.’”

ref: The king answered, “Go to Canaan and keep your promise to your father.”
text: Naaba maa yeli u, “Wa ti pi e ba, a maa seema o n ti e digili noori la.”

ref: After crossing the Jordan River, Joseph stopped at Atad's threshing place, where they all mourned and wept seven days for Jacob.
text: Ba n paa Atadi chibuura gbantali, a leeli Joodan mogiri, ba chaari kpeeŋutiri, a ni sufusaaŋu. Nisaaniŋi n na, Yisifu naa dabaayapoin kumi o ba kuuri.

ref: So Jacob's sons did just as their father had instructed.
text: Lala n na, Yakubu biisi niŋi seema ba ba n yeli ba.

ref: They took him to Mamre in Canaan and buried him in Machpelah Cave, the burial place Abraham had bought from Ephron the Hittite.
text: Ba zi u gaa Keenan tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, a wa ti pi u yi zovoori n ba Makipela tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi, a leeli Mamiri, so Ibrahim n daa n da, o niriba piibu ziya, a yi Efuron n niŋi Hititi nira saani, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu maa.

ref: After the funeral, Joseph, his brothers, and everyone else returned to Egypt. Joseph's Promise to His Brothers
text: Yisifu n pi o ba baasi, o ni o mabiisi ni seesi zaa n paa ba gaa la pori n gaa Yijipiti. Yisifu che n paŋŋi o beya

ref: After Jacob died, Joseph's brothers said to each other, “What if Joseph still hates us and wants to get even with us for all the cruel things we did to him?”
text: Ba ba kum nyaaŋa, Yisifu mabiisi la yeli taaba, “Saŋa, Yisifu ni chisi ti, a yo ti yabeseesi ti n daa n niŋi u la samini.”

ref: So they sent this message to Joseph: Before our father died,
text: A zu, ba tumi nira Yisifu saani, a wa ti yeli u. “Seema e ba n yeli ti, ka kpi n ŋa,

ref: he told us, “You did some cruel and terrible things to Joseph, but you must ask him to forgive you.” Now we ask you to please forgive the terrible things we did. After all, we serve the same God that your father worshiped. When Joseph heard this, he started crying.
text: ‘Ya yeli Yisifu n ŋa: M suusira i, e che n paŋŋi e mabiisaa, yabeseesi ni namsigu seesi zaa ba n daa n niŋi i la.’ A zu, jaande, che n paŋŋi e ba Naawunni gurunsi ba yabeeri.” Ba yatogiraa n paa Yisifu, o kumi.

ref: Right then, Joseph's brothers came and bowed down to the ground in front of him and said, “We are your slaves.”
text: Niŋi n na, o mabiisi maa ti li n do o yiya, ka yeli u, “E daaba n niŋi ti.”

ref: But Joseph told them, “Don't be afraid! I have no right to change what God has decided.
text: Yisifu yeli ba, “Ya da zora dabeem, m bi niŋi Naawunni.

ref: You tried to harm me, but God made it turn out for the best, so that he could save all these people, as he is now doing.
text: Ya wune zani ya niŋi ma daaŋu, ama Naawunni wune naag u loobi binsuŋŋu, o ti niŋi bunso n niŋi moligirisim ti nivudamta nyevuya.

ref: Don't be afraid! I will take care of you and your children.” After Joseph said this, his brothers felt much better. Joseph's Death
text: Lala zu, ya da zora dabeem, m ni nyaasi ya ni ya biisi.” O togsi yatogsuma ti ba, a maa ba sufusi. Yisifu kum yela

ref: Joseph lived in Egypt with his brothers until he died at the age of
text: Yisifu daa n zinni Yijipiti, o ni o ba biisi zaa. O daa n dini yuma koowa ni piiya.

ref: Joseph lived long enough to see Ephraim's children and grandchildren. He also lived to see the children of Manasseh's son Machir, and he welcomed them into his family.
text: O zi n nye Efirem biisi ni yaŋsi. Ka Manaasi bii Makiya gba daa n dogi biisi, ba naa ba pa Yisifu noba niŋi.

ref: Before Joseph died, he told his brothers, “I won't live much longer. But God will take care of you and lead you out of Egypt to the land he promised Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Niŋi n na, Yisifu yeli o mabiisi maa, “M kum jemani paaya. Ama, Naawunni ni paasi ya niŋi, a nyisi ya kpe gaa tiŋgbanso o n digili noori ti Ibrahim, Yisaaka ni Yakubu la niŋi.”

ref: Now promise me that you will take my body with you when God leads you to that land.”
text: Yisifu ti Yakubu biisi pe, a yeli maa, “Jemanso Naawunni n nyisi ya kpe, a wara tiŋgbaŋŋu maa niŋi, ya naa m koba yi kpe paasi n wara.”
